case_records.issue: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
case_records.narrative:
I am filing this complaint because XXXX is refusing to open or properly review a dispute for merchandise that was never delivered. 

I made a purchase from XXXX using XXXX. The package was never delivered, and XXXX officially confirmed in writing that they were unable to locate the package and that it is considered lost. 

Despite providing this proof, XXXX closed my case and refused to issue a refund or offer any resolution. 

When I attempted to open a dispute with XXXX, the app automatically rejected my request and did not allow me to explain the situation or upload documentation. XXXX also sent an automated response stating they are not responsible, without conducting a formal review. 
As a result, I am being required to pay for merchandise I never received, even with official proof from the courier confirming the loss.

case_records.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
case_records.issue: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
case_records.narrative:
I purchased XXXX shoes using Klarnas Pay in 4 service and fully paid the order. I returned the shoes to XXXX, and XXXX issued a refund to Klarna. 

Despite my original bank account being active, unchanged, and in good standing, Klarnas system converted the refund into Klarna credit and applied it to other balances on my account without my authorization.

I contacted Klarna via their app and requested a manual refund to my bank account. They replied that the refund could not be linked to my original payment plan and that the automated system could not be changed. Klarna acknowledged the issue and assigned complaint ID XXXX, but the refund has still not been returned to my bank account. 

This is a technical error on Klarnas side and does not relieve them of the obligation to return my funds to my original payment method, as required under Pay in 4 refund policy.

I have attached proof.

case_records.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
case_records.issue: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
case_records.narrative:
I am disputing a {$1200.00} charge related to airfare purchased through XXXX XXXX  ( XXXX ). 

In XX/XX/year>, before the first flight date, I explicitly asked the merchant whether I could cancel the first flight while retaining the return flight. The merchant responded but failed to disclose that missing or cancelling the first flight would automatically cancel the return segment. 

Relying on the incomplete information provided, I kept the ticket unchanged as I intended to use the return flight. I later learnedmonths afterward and only upon my own inquirythat the entire itinerary, including the return flight, had already been canceled without my knowledge. As a result, the travel service I paid for could not be used as intended due to the merchants failure to disclose the cancellation consequence. 

I disputed the charge with American Express on the basis of merchant misrepresentation by omission and services not rendered. The dispute was denied, and the charge was rebilled.

case_records.issue: Problem with a purchase or transfer
case_records.narrative:
I am disputing {$690.00} in charges on a prepaid XXXX card issued by Fifth Third Bank. I submitted a formal dispute by email on XX/XX/XXXX, and when I called the next day the company confirmed the dispute was received. 
Dieting that call, I was told that no action would be taken for up to XXXX business days. I was refused access to a supervisor, refused information about how to file a complaint, and the representative ended the call. 
I believe this is an unreasonable delay and improper handling of a dispute. I am requesting provisional credit and a good-faith investigation consistent with consumer protection requirements. 

Additionally, this prepaid card was issued as part of a clinical trial, and I was explicitly told by the customer service representative at [ XXXX ] to go back to the research facility instead of assisting me. This is clearly just an attempt to give me the runaround and avoid resolving the dispute properly.

The branch staff, including the assistant branch manager, stated that they could not help and had no information about the status of the card. I was repeatedly told to wait without any results. 

This situation caused me a loss of time and unnecessary stress. Additionally, the hard credit inquiry related to this account negatively impacted my credit score, even though I never used the account or received the card. 

The bank failed to provide basic service and failed to resolve a simple issue over an extended period of time.

case_records.narrative: To the Account Review Team, I am writing to formally request a manual review and reversal of a {$240.00} interest charge assessed to my XXXX  XXXX Business credit card. Due to a brief administrative oversight, my payment was delayed by only 3 days. As soon as the error was noticed, the balance was immediately paid in full. This account is otherwise kept in excellent standing, and this minor delay is an isolated incident. When I contacted phone support, a manager informed me that the automated system issued a denial. However, as a loyal Bank of America client who holds multiple active XXXX XXXX credit cards, a {$240.00} penalty for a 72-hour oversight severely and unfairly impacts my business expenses. I am a new small business. I kindly request that a human review team override the automated system decision and credit the {$240.00} back to my account as a gesture of goodwill to preserve our ongoing business relationship.Thank you for your time, consideration, and prompt attention to this matter.

assessment.why_concerning: CRITICAL severity: 145 consumers affected by the same pattern at BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION and others, and it is accelerating fast (+400% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, artificial intelligence, automated loop, automated system.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 145 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated account-closure / fraud model appears to be exiting customers and reversing payments without manual review or notice.
hypotheses.evidence: 145 complaints showing automated closure and payment-reversal cues; concentrated at BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORPORATION, CITIBANK, N.A..

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Consider commissioning a Skilled Person review of the firm's automated-decisioning governance and oversight.

I am filing a complaint regarding TD Banks handling of an unauthorized ATM withdrawal dispute involving {$5900.00} stolen from my account. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, around XXXX XXXX, my wallet and phone were stolen from me in XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX. When I woke up later that morning and logged into the TD Bank website, I discovered that all of my funds had been withdrawn from my TD Bank accounts through multiple ATM transactions totaling {$5900.00}. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I went to TD Bank and filed a fraud dispute for the unauthorized ATM withdrawals. This is on top of the fact that a police report was given to the bank to amplify the fact that I did not authorize the transaction. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a denial letter from TD Bank stating the transactions were considered authorized solely because the correct PIN was used at a TD ATM. This reasoning is incorrect under Regulation E. A transaction is not considered authorized simply because the thief used my physical card and PIN after stealing my wallet and phone. 

Because the banks explanation contradicted federal law, I immediately went back to TD Bank and requested that the claim be reopened. While TD did reopen the case, they have taken no

case_records.issue: Problem with a lender or other company charging your account
case_records.narrative:
XXXX XXXX. closed my merchant account ( Merchant ID XXXX ) without explanation and began withdrawing money from my business account without my authorization. An amount of {$1400.00} was withdrawn 10 times. I contacted them, but they did not provide any reason or solution.

I am requesting that XXXX return all withdrawn money and stop interfering with my business. I will no longer use their services and can not recommend them.

I am not disputing the closure decision because I no longer want to bank with a bank who close accounts for no reason, I only request the timely return of my funds or to be able to get the restriction lifted long enough for me to go to an atm and withdraw the funds.

What Regions is doing : They have blocked all access to my funds, including the remaining balance of more than {$60000.00}. I can not pay my bills and they have returned all payment. I am wrongfully having to pay fees and thousands of dollars on returned items to restore accounts. They have wreaked havoc on my life.

They refuse to tell me why the acco

This restriction has effectively frozen my account and prevented me from accessing my own funds, causing financial stress and limiting my ability to meet obligations. The decision was made unilaterally and after my deposit, without prior notice of such a hold period. 

I have searched onli

case_records.issue: Problem caused by your funds being low
case_records.narrative:
Between XX/XX/year>, my bank authorized multiple debit card transactions while the accounts available balance was displayed as positive in the mobile banking application. On XX/XX/year>, the mobile app showed an available balance of approximately {$43.00}, and transactions were not declined or blocked. 

A debit card transfer involving {$100.00}, which had been authorized earlier, later posted after additional debit activity had already been approved. Following the posting of this transaction, the bank assessed multiple {$36.00} overdraft fees, even though the transactions had been authorized using the available balance shown at the time. 

When I contacted the bank for clarification, I was told that overdraft determinations are based on the available balance, not the current balance. This conflicted with the fact that the transactions had already been authorized and completed based on the available balance displayed in the mobile application. 

After my inquiry, the bank refunded XXXX overdraft fees totaling {$72.00}, describing it as a one-time courtesy. No explanation was provided as to why only some overdraft fees were refunded while others were retained, and my questions regar

I reported unauthorized transactions on my Cash App account that I did not authorize, initiate, or benefit from. I timely disputed these transactions and requested an investigation under Regulation E. 

Cash App denied the dispute without providing evidence of authorization and claimed that possession of my phone constituted authorization, which is not valid under Regulation E. Cash App did not provide the results of any investigation or explain how the transactions were determined to be authorized. 

During the dispute, Cash App closed my account and stated disputes could not proceed while the account was closed. Cash App later confirmed in writing that my account was reinstated and active, but the app again showed the account as deactivated, declined transactions, and restricted access without explanation. 

Cash App states my case must be handled by a specialty or dispute department that has no direct phone number or email and communicates only through one-way messages that can not be replied to. Customer support says they must contact this department internally and instructs me to wait for emails I can not respond to. 

Cash App has failed to provide evidence of authorization,

On XX/XX/year>, I authorized a bill pay payment of {$620.00} to XXXX XXXX. The funds were deducted from my account and cleared. 

I have repeatedly requested formal proof of settlement or a completed payment trace from US bank, as XXXX XXXX  states they have not received this payment or information provided to them. 

Every time I call I get the run around that XXXX doesn't handle this or US bank doesn't handle this. 

This dispute has been going on for XXXX months now.

In late XX/XX/2024, XXXX Bank of America checking account, which I had maintained for approximately XXXX years, was closed by the bank without any prior notice or warning. I did not request the closure. 

After the account was closed, I contacted Bank of America by phone. I was informed that the bank made the decision to close the account but was not provided with any reason. I was told that the remaining balance in my account would be mailed to me in the form of a check within a couple of weeks. 

By the last week of XXXX, I had not received the check, so I contacted Bank of America again. At that time, I was told that the account was still pending closing and that it should be fully closed by XX/XX/XXXX. 

I contacted Bank of America again on XX/XX/XXXX, and again on XX/XX/XXXX, and each time I was told that the account was still pending closing. As of today, more than XXXX months after the bank closed my account, I have not received my remaining funds, nor have I been given a clear explanation for the delay. 

Despite repeated phone calls, Bank of America has been unable or unwilling to provide : A reason for the account closure A firm date for when my funds will be released Con

I am filing a complaint regarding Cash Apps wrongful account closure, ongoing refusal to provide access to my investment assets, and the unlawful blacklisting of my identity. 

Cash App closed my original account on XX/XX/XXXX, claiming activity that violated their Terms of Service. I requested clarification repeatedly and was never provided ANY explanation, example, or evidence of wrongdoing. I did not engage in any fraudulent or suspicious behavior. I used the account ONLY to receive my work direct deposits and send/receive transfers between my husband and children.

Cash App never provided a path to challenge the closure, correct incorrect information, or dispute any flag on my identity. This violates basic consumer-rights expectations and their responsibility to provide a dispute process. 

The larger problem is that Cash App continues to hold investment assets that belong to me.

I still hold stocks through Cash App, including XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, and XXXX, as well as XXXX purchased before my account was closed. In XX/XX/XXXX, Cash App sent me a shareholder notice about upcoming votes related to XXXX which confirms that they still recognize me as the legal owner of these asse

Robinhood has restricted my account and is preventing me from accessing my legal funds. My phone number on file is outdated, and the automated system provides no way to update it while the account is restricted. I have attempted to resolve this through support, but I am stuck in an automated loop. I am ready to provide Government ID and a Selfie for manual verification immediately. I am requesting that a human representative contact me at ( XXXX ) XXXX or provide a secure link to upload my ID so I can regain access to my money. 
The company 's security protocols have created a Catch-22 where I am unable to verify my identity because the system only allows verification through a phone number I no longer own.

My US Citi bank account was terminated suddenly by the bank without prior notice. I realized that when I was opening my Citi mobile App and it had a reference code XXXX and to call customer service.When I contacted customer service to ask for the reason, I was told that the explanation would be provided by mail only, sent to the address on file, which is outside the United States.

I asked whether the explanation could be sent to an alternative address, as I am currently outside the U.S. The representative stated that the request would be noted but could not guarantee which address would be used.

I also asked about the return of my remaining funds. I was informed that : My funds would be returned by mailed check The estimated timeframe would be 3060 days No approximate issuance date could be provided No confirmation of the amount could be provided No reference or case number could be shared I was told that access to my account was fully blocked, making it impossible for me to verify my own balance or transaction history. I was unable to obtain confirmation of how much money is being held or when it will be released. 

This is particularly concerning because I previously had multip

This email serves as formal notice of Bank of Americas deceptive practices, predatory account-retention mechanisms, and deliberate obstruction of lawful account closure. What follows is not a customer service complaint. It is a record of systemic misconduct. 
Bank of Americas rewards system displayed a balance of {$390.00} associated with my Visa Signature card. That figure was presented as real, available value. Any reasonable consumer would understand this to represent transferable funds. Acting on that representation, I opened a checking account for the sole purpose of accessing what Bank of Americas own interface indicated I possessed. 
Only after the account was opened did the truth emerge : the {$390.00} figure was illusory. The actual balance was XXXX reward points, effectively worthless. This is not a misunderstanding. It is a deceptive presentation of value embedded in your systems. Had Bank of America accurately represented the nature of those rewards, the checking account would never have been opened. 
What followed escalates this from deception to coercion. 
Bank of America has constructed its systems so that a checking account can not be closed through any normal, mode

I requested a dispute for a refund of {$21.00} that was processed on XXXX from XXXX. 
They only sent me {$12.00} for a previous amount from the same transaction. Ive reached out numerous times and keep getting the run around. They closed the case. Im not able to speak to a XXXX or XXXX. Im not able to reopen the dispute either. This is getting so frustrating. I dont think they understand that there was XXXX refunds processed for the same transaction. The total amount was {$36.00}. I received the first refund for {$12.00}. But the second refund was processed for the other item on XXXX for {$21.00}. I never received it and they said it was pending. After XXXX I waited and still didnt receive it so started a dispute. Well they closed the case yesterday and only processed {$12.00}. But it should have been {$21.00}.

I bank with Citibank and have been for 2 years. There automated system I call in to get all my transaction history is not working and when I call in it says no transaction in the last 6 months. Its been down since XXXX XXXX. I have called every day and my balance is off. They keep telling me thats its being worked on and there maintenance going on but there is nothing posted on Citibank website any where. I need assistance and legally they are withholding my transaction history and request me to go into bank or online. I dont do online banking. Please help

I am filing a formal complaint against TD Bank regarding a missing {$1000.00} cash deposit and a complete lack of transparency or resolution from branch management and corporate customer service. 

Incident Details : On XX/XX/year>, I visited the TD Bank branch in XXXX XXXX, XXXX  to make a deposit of {$1900.00} in cash and one check for {$120.00}. The teller recorded the check and only {$900.00} of the cash. The remaining {$1000.00} in cash never appeared in my account. 

Timeline and Bank 's Inaction : - Immediate Notification : I notified the branch management of the discrepancy immediately. 
- Repeated Follow-ups : I have visited the branch weekly since XX/XX/year>, seeking a status update. Management consistently promised a written/email update but has provided zero documentation or communication to date. 
- Runaround : Calling the main TD Bank customer service resulted in being redirected back to the unhelpful branch staff. 
- Unacceptable Excuses : I was told the issue would be fixed in 10 days ( it was not ). I was then told the investigation can not proceed until the specific teller returns from vacation, which is an unacceptable delay mechanism for locating missing funds.

case_records.issue: Problem with a lender or other company charging your account
case_records.narrative:
I enrolled in XXXX Ads under a promotional offer advertised as {$50.00} per month, which I understood and was willing to pay. I initially observed a {$50.00} charge that aligned with the promotion. 

A few weeks later, my bank notified me of a large charge of approximately {$600.00} from XXXX. This charge was automatically flagged and cancelled by my bank due to its unusually high amount. This was the first time I became aware that the charges had increased beyond the XXXX XXXX  monthly amount I authorized. 

Immediately after being notified of this charge, I contacted XXXX by phone to cancel the service. I also contacted Bank of America to dispute the charge. Bank of America informed me that the transactions were unauthorized and stated that the merchant would be blocked. 

At XXXX point, I observed that the XXXX transaction was completely removed from my account, and I was told the merchant had been blocked. Based on this, I believed the issue had been fully resolved. 

Despite this, XXXX later attempted and processed additional charges, and the total amount increased further to approximately {$680.00}, even after the cancellation and after I was told the merchant was blocked. I

" I am writing to formally express my dissatisfaction with the handling of my account reopening request. I previously held an account with Charles Schwab, which was closed in 2019. Recently, I attempted to reopen the account, following all required steps. However, due to the previous address associated with the account, my request was escalated to the fraud department. I complied fully, submitting all requested information and forms. Despite my efforts, I received no follow-up and felt I was given the runaround. When I reached out again, I was informed that my application was denied and that I can reapply in six months without a clear explanation. There was no indication of fraudulent activity ; the issue seemed to be solely related to the address. I believe this situation has been handled unfairly, and I request a thorough review of my case with the aim of reopening my account. I would appreciate a prompt response to clarify the next steps. ''

Subject : Regulatory Complaint - Systemic Failure of Fraud Detection ( XXXX XXXX Violation ) & Unfair Practices I am reporting a gross failure of Bank of America 's security protocols regarding my business account, which resulted in a total loss of {$11000.00}.

1. Total Failure of Suspicious Activity Monitoring : My Online Banking dashboard ( " Top Outbound Cash Destinations '' ) confirms that a total of {$11000.00} was transferred to " KALSHI '' ( a high-risk gambling merchant ) without my authorization.

The fraudulent attacks began in late XXXX ( see attached XXXX XXXX ) and escalated to XXXX consecutive transactions on XX/XX/XXXX alone. 

No commercially reasonable security system should permit XXXX repetitive transfers to a new gambling merchant in XXXX hours without triggering a block or a single fraud alert. This suggests the bank 's AML and Fraud filters were non-functional. 

XXXX. Failure to Protect Deposits ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : On XX/XX/XXXX, I made a legitimate mobile deposit of {$4900.00} ( Statement Page XXXX ). Immediately after accepting my money, the bank 's system allowed XXXX more fraudulent transfers to XXXX on the very same day, instantly draining the funds I just

In XXXX  they limited my account with XXXX dollars in it. I had never had the chance to even use my PayPal account they froze it immediately after I had the deposit made. They only thing I've ever been told was that I never used PayPal before so the fact that I had a XXXX XXXX dollar deposit with no background use with them it set the algorithm off and to 180 days to get my funds back that was 5 years ago with absolutely no help and refusal of funds transfer and told they can not tell me why they won't release my money.

Upon receiving approximately {$100.00} in XXXX, which is not out of the ordinary for my account over the past 3 years without any issues, PayPal has restricted me from using that {$100.00}. Which is used for personal bills like gas and groceries. I requested a reason for why, and which information they are using from my personal records, but the PayPal employee failed to disclose any information about which the institution was using. This is a major red flag warning for consumer protection and I believe it is within the best interest of the people to report the improper and I would delegate, illegal use of information via Artificial Intelligence and automation transaction type programming.

Truist isnt doing anything to help me with this transaction. I have been having issues with Truist bank for a long time now and that has forced me to change banks. Debit card transactions taking 4 to 5 days to even show up in my online banking. Incorrect dates of those transactions ( covering up their laziness ) That has caused me sig

I was questioned to determine whether the account compromise and fraudulent transactions occured due to my cooperation being tricked or scammed or whether they occured entirely absent of my permission or awareness. The agent noted the account changes to email, phone number and password had all been changed and that the changes occured from a location far from my own on a different device on XX/XX/XXXX. With my credentials, security and settings compromised i remained unaware of the fraud until it was discovered as this accounts only purpose is to process paper check deposits and tax returns. This was done on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX : {$28.00}, {$120.00}, {$120.00}, {$100.00}, {$100.00}. A dispute against the unauthorized charges was made with the agent upon Identifying the above fraud ( XXXX pm XX/XX/XXXX claim number : XXXX ) Once the fraud was identified, reported and a dispute filed i was then placed with an account specialist to ensure my account information was accurate and secured with all elements of fraud reported. It should be noted here both agents confi

I opened a new checking account with Citibank less than 30 days ago. My account currently holds approximately {$4000.00} of my personal funds. Citibank has blocked my account and is currently denying me access to my funds due to a failure in their internal identity verification systems. 

The primary basis of this complaint is a significant data integrity error by the bank. Citibank is attempting to verify my identity using a phone number that is not mine and has never been associated with my account. Despite the fact that I have a verified phone number on filewhich the bank has successfully used to verify me in the pastthe banks 'back-office ' system is pulling 'ghost ' data from an unknown third-party source and insisting I verify via a number I do not own. 

I have spent numerous hours on the phone with Citibanks Fraud and Account Protection departments. Because their automated system is pulling incorrect data, the agents claim they ' can not verify ' me to lift the block. The only solution offered is to wait 710 business days for a reference code to be sent via physical mail. 

Requiring a customer to wait two weeks to access {$4000.00} because of the bank 's own inability to m

I am submitting this complaint regarding Navy Federal Credit Unions delay of funds availability and account restrictions on my checking account. 

I opened this account separately and had no unresolved disputes on it. Despite this, my paycheck that was scheduled to be available on my normal pay date was delayed until a later date without explanation. 

When I contacted Navy Federal Credit Union, the automated system referenced a negative account that does not belong to this account and is tied to a separate unresolved dispute on a related member account. 

Navy Federal Credit Union has not provided a clear explanation or justification for delaying my wages or linking my account to a separate disputed matter. This has interfered with my access to earned wages and caused financial harm. 

I am requesting immediate availability of my funds and confirmation that my account will not be restricted or affected by disputes on other accounts.

On XX/XX/XXXX, I went to make a {$3000.00} transaction. I called ahead and was told there would be no issue. My daily limit is {$5000.00}. Despite this, the transaction was flagged for fraud. 

I called immediately and was placed on hold for an excessive amount of time. I was told I was under a fraud investigation and that it could take up to XXXX  days. I then went to the branch in XXXX XXXX the following day with my passport and ID as I was told to do so by the customer service rep. I was told they needed to call the fraud department and that it would take time. 

I was then given the same response as over the phone : no explanation, no additional information, a blanket statement that it could take up to XXXX days, and I was told to leave the branch. 

This is unacceptable. I recently deposited {$8000.00} from my XXXX XXXX XXXX benefits, funds I had already been waiting weeks to receive. The bank is now holding money I need immediately to cover my mortgage and other essential bills. I can not wait XXXX days. This situation is causing serious financial harm, and the lack of communication or urgency is completely unreasonable.

After liquidating certain positions in my account and attempting to withdraw my funds, Robinhood automatically restricted my account from making withdrawals. I requested an account review, but instead of resolving the issue, my account was further restricted. All of my remaining positions are now locked, and I am unable to trade or sell them at needed prices. I also can not access my funds, which I need to pay essential bills and expenses. Robinhood provided an estimated resolution date of XX/XX/XXXX ; however, based on numerous customer reviews, the company has a pattern of repeatedly pushing back estimated resolution dates, causing prolonged financial harm. My account deposits and witdraws have always cleared, and there is no pattern of misuse of NSF/chargebacks on my account.

In XX/XX/XXXX, I opened a Schwab Investor Checking account. Schwab verified my external XXXX XXXX  account through two microdeposits, which I confirmed. I completed an initial {$50.00} transfer from the same XXXX XXXX account without issue. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I initiated an ACH transfer of approximately {$900.00} from the same verified XXXX XXXX account into my Schwab account. Later that day, I enrolled in XXXX. Immediately after enrollment, Schwab suspended my XXXX access without explanation. I contacted Schwab and was told the XXXX department was closed and that a message would be sent to them. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, my Schwab account remained accessible and the {$900.00} transfer displayed a standard hold. On XX/XX/XXXX, I was unexpectedly locked out of my XXXXchwab account entirely. When I called Schwab, I was told the lockout was due to a need to verify that the funds came from my actual account, even though Schwab had already verified this same external account in XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I went to a Schwab branch and provided all requested documents, including government ID, my XXXX XXXX  XXXX  statement, a XXXX  XXXX  support email, and printed transaction records. Because the transf

I am submitting this complaint regarding OceanFirst Banks denial of a promotional checking account bonus after I satisfied all disclosed requirements. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, I received notice of an OceanFirst Bank promotion offering a {$500.00} bonus for opening a qualifying checking account and completing specified direct deposits. Relying on the terms of that promotion, I opened a Freestyle+ Checking Account on XX/XX/XXXX, and funded the account with {$50.00}.

case_records.narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to formally dispute a continuous pattern of excessive, compounding, and predatory automated overdraft/NSF fees applied to my checking account ( ending in XXXX ) between XX/XX/2026, and XX/XX/2026. I have attached the complete 60-day transaction history highlighting these specific errors and am requesting a comprehensive audit and courtesy reversal by a corporate manager.

I attempted to resolve this locally by speaking with the manager at the XXXX XXXX. The manager refused to help me, dismissively told me I was wrong, and stated that everything processed by the bank was correct. She claimed that the fees are triggered because I am being charged based on the total negative balance of the account rather than per individual transaction item.

While I now understand that your automated system utilizes the total negative balance at the end of the day to override individual item protections, the way these charges stack up creates an inescapable trap. Please review the following blatant issues clearly visible in the attached screenshots : Predatory Double-Charging on Single Returned Items ( XX/XX/1314 ) : On XX/XX/XXXX, a {$600.00} Chase transfer attem

assessment.why_concerning: HIGH severity: 99 consumers affected by the same pattern at BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION and others, and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, artificial intelligence, automated loop, automated system.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 99 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: The firm likely tightened an automated transaction-monitoring / financial-crime model, raising false-positive exit rates — legitimate customers are 'de-risked' and offboarded without manual review.
hypotheses.evidence: 99 complaints showing account-closure and suspicious-activity language concentrated at a few firms; concentrated at BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, Chime Financial Inc, Block, Inc..

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated fraud-detection model may be flagging legitimate transactions, freezing funds before any human check.
hypotheses.evidence: 99 complaints showing fund-freeze and access-loss cues; concentrated at BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, Chime Financial Inc, Block, Inc..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

assessment.why_concerning: HIGH severity: 62 consumers affected by the same pattern at Chime Financial Inc and others, and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: artificial intelligence, automated system, glitch.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 62 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: The firm likely tightened an automated transaction-monitoring / financial-crime model, raising false-positive exit rates — legitimate customers are 'de-risked' and offboarded without manual review.
hypotheses.evidence: 62 complaints showing account-closure and suspicious-activity language concentrated at a few firms; concentrated at Chime Financial Inc, Block, Inc., WELLS FARGO & COMPANY.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated fraud-detection model may be flagging legitimate transactions, freezing funds before any human check.
hypotheses.evidence: 62 complaints showing fund-freeze and access-loss cues; concentrated at Chime Financial Inc, Block, Inc., WELLS FARGO & COMPANY.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Chime Financial Inc for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Chime Financial Inc directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

sample_narratives:
I am complaining about Discovers handling of my credit limit and the accuracy of its adverse action notices for my Discover card ending in XXXX. 

I have held this account for approximately ten years. It was originally opened as a {$2000.00} cash-secured card and later converted to an unsecured card. Despite this long history and regular use, my credit limit has remained at {$2000.00} the entire time. 

I am a XXXX consumer on long-term disability income. Across my credit file I currently have approximately XXXX active accounts/tradelines, including this Discover card. Discovers own information has shown my score in the XXXX ( around XXXX ). I requested a credit limit increase and received a denial letter from Discover dated XX/XX/XXXX. The reasons listed were : 1. Balances on bankcard or revolving accts high compared to credit limits 2. The date that you opened your oldest account is too recent 3. Balance amount paid down on your open installment accounts is too low I understand that utilization and installment balances may affect decisions. My concern is with reason # 2. This Discover account is roughly a decade old and began as a secured card. It is one of my oldest tradelines among the XXXX accounts on my report. Describing my oldest account as too recent appears inaccurate or misleading and suggests a problem with either Discovers data or its automated reason-code system. 

In addition, keeping this account at the original {$2000.00} limit for roughly XXXX years has significantly reduced its real buying power. According to published Consumer Price Index data, prices in the U.S. increased by about 36.69 % between XXXX and XXXX, meaning that {$1.00} in XXXX has the same buying power as approximately {$1.00} in XXXX. A {$2000.00} limit set around XXXX would need to be about {$2700.00} in XXXX to maintain the same purchasing power. By leaving my limit at {$2000.00}, Discover has effectively reduced the real value of this line by roughly 27 %, even before considerin

am complaining about Capital Ones handling of my credit limits and its treatment of me as a XXXX customer. 

I currently have three Capital One credit cards, each with a credit limit of {$1000.00}. These accounts have been open for several years and I have used them responsibly. Despite this, Capital One has kept all three cards at the same starter-level {$1000.00} limit and has repeatedly declined requests for increases. 

I am a XXXX consumer on long-term XXXX income, with a credit file that includes approximately XXXX active tradelines across multiple institutions. My overall profile is not that of a thin-file or first-time borrower. Nevertheless, Capital One continues to treat all three of my accounts as if I am permanently stuck in an entry-level tier. 

When I request credit line increases, Capital One either denies the requests outright or provides only generic, boilerplate reasons ( for example, references to internal score or high utilization ) without any clear explanation of why three long-tenured accounts must remain at {$1000.00} each. There is no transparent path for these accounts to graduate into normal limits consistent with my history. 

In addition, Capital One recently approved me for a XXXX XXXX card but assigned it a credit limit of only {$300.00}. XXXX XXXX is marketed as a premium travel card typically associated with substantially higher limits and meaningful purchasing power. Issuing a premium-branded card with a {$300.00} limit, while keeping my three older Capital One cards at {$1000.00} each, reinforces my concern that Capital Ones systems are keeping me in a permanent low-limit tier regardless of product type, history, or overall profile. The branding and product type change, but the underlying algorithm appears to treat me as a permanently constrained, high-yield, low-limit customer. 

My concern is not only the low nominal limits, but the long-term erosion of real value and access. Over the past decade, inflation has significantly red

On XX/XX/year>, I received an adverse action notice from Capital One denying my request for credit. The specific denial reason provided was : " Capital One has observed activity on one or more of your Capital One accounts that is inconsistent with typical customer account usage '' This denial is unfair and relies on outdated internal data rather than my current creditworthiness. The " activity '' referenced refers to a single secured credit card account from over four years ago. That account is closed, and I do not owe Capital One any money.

Since that time, I have successfully rebuilt my credit profile and established a successful small business. Despite my current high credit score and clean history with other lenders, Capital One is using a " lifetime ban '' algorithm based on a resolved, 4-year-old secured account to automatically deny me services.

By refusing to look at my actual credit report and instead relying on a permanent internal " blacklist, '' the bank is failing to assess my true creditworthiness.

case_records.narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Synchrony Banks reduction of my credit limit immediately after I utilized my credit line following direct marketing and solicitation from Synchrony. 

Synchrony actively marketed and encouraged me to use my available credit. In reliance on this solicitation, I used the credit line as promoted. Shortly thereafter, Synchrony reduced my credit limit to reflect approximately 90 % utilization, which materially increased my utilization ratio and caused a steep decline in my credit score. 

Synchrony advised that this adverse action was based on an automated algorithm citing the following factors : Bank card balance-to-income ratio Allegedly insufficient payment amounts Loan balances This constitutes an adverse action under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), 15 U.S.C. 1681a ( k ), and triggers specific notice, accuracy, and disclosure obligations.

The stated reasons for the adverse action are inconsistent with my documented credit profile and raise concerns regarding compliance with FCRA 1681e ( b ) and 1681m ( a ) : My credit report reflects 100 % on-time payments across all credit accounts All payments on the Synchrony account were made on

case_records.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
case_records.narrative:
I am writing to file an official complaint regarding Capital Ones handling of two secured credit card applications, security deposits, and subsequent account closures, which I believe constitute deceptive, unethical, and potentially criminal business practices. 

In XX/XX/year>, I applied for a Capital One secured credit card using my Capital One checking account. Capital One withdrew the required security deposit and confirmed that the account had been opened. However, before I ever received the card or had the opportunity to use the account, Capital One abruptly closed it without justification or notice. 

Shortly thereafter, I received another mail offer from Capital One for a secured credit card. I submitted a second application, and again, Capital One immediately withdrew my security deposit from my Capital One checking account and opened the account. Once again, before the card ever arrived, Capital One closed the account without cause or explanation. 

I have now been informed that it will take up to 69 days to receive a refund of the funds that were taken directly from my Capital One checking account. This is unacceptable and appears to be intentional misconduct.

Capital O

Comenity Capital Bank abruptly closed multiple of my newly opened store credit card accounts ( XXXX XXXX, Saks Fifth Avenue, and Carters ) within days of opening them, without providing clear or specific reasons. All accounts were closed at the same time and labeled as previous negative experience, even though I had no late payments, no delinquencies, and no issues with these accounts. I believe Comenity acted unfairly and without proper explanation. I was never notified of any problem, and all accounts were in good standing. Closing multiple accounts at once without justification, and then reporting them as credit grantor closed account, is damaging, misleading, and negatively affects my ability to obtain credit.

- " Average Time Since Accounts Opened '' : My oldest account is 7+ years old.

- " Bankcard Balances '' : the high balance on a single card issued by XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX is due to strategic balance transfers. That card, like every one of my cards, is in good standing and paid on time. It is literally none of Synchrony 's business and a clear indication their reasoning is without merits. If XXXX  XXXX XXXX is ok with our arrangement, Synchrony can not hold that against me. 
- " Too Few Installment Accounts Recently Paid As Agreed '' : Th

I am filing this complaint against Synchrony Bank regarding the unexpected and unexplained closure of my XXXX XXXX & XXXX credit card account. The account was closed without my knowledge, without my consent, and without any notice being received. This closure appears inconsistent, arbitrary, and potentially in violation of federal consumerprotection laws. 

I only discovered the closure after the fact. I never received any Adverse Action Notice, as required under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ( ECOA ) and Regulation B ( 12 CFR 1002.9 ). If Synchrony claims a notice was sent, it was never delivered, and I am requesting proof of mailing, including the date, method, and address used. The lack of notice deprived me of my right to understand the reason for the closure or to dispute any underlying issue. 

What makes this situation even more concerning is that I have another active Synchrony Bank credit card account that was not closed for inactivity. This inconsistency suggests that the closure of the XXXX XXXX account was not based on a uniform policy or legitimate business reason. It raises the possibility of an internal error, misclassification, or improper adverse action.

Unfair and Deceptive Payment Allocation Practices by Synchrony Bank I am filing this complaint to formally report an unfair, deceptive, and systematically abusive payment allocation practice by Synchrony Bank regarding their credit card accounts with multiple deferred-interest promotional balances.

I currently hold a Synchrony credit card account that has multiple overlapping promotional purchases on it ( e.g., an older, higher-amount equal-payment promotion and multiple newer XXXX deferred interest promotions ). The newer promotions are for smaller amounts and will expire a year earlier than the older promotion.

Synchrony 's payment system defaults to an internal allocation algorithm. Crucially, Synchrony deliberately provides no online tool or interface that allows consumers to manually route their excess payments to the promotional balance that will incur retroactive interest the soonest.

To avoid thousands of dollars in retroactive interest, I am forced to call Synchrony Bank 's customer service every single month to manually override their system and direct my payment to the correct balance. To make matters worse, if a consumer does not get through to a representative soon

I am filing a formal complaint against Synchrony Bank regarding the sudden and unexplained closure of three of my credit card accounts : CareCredit, XXXX XXXX XXXX, and XXXX XXXX XXXX  credit card. 

On XX/XX/year>, I attempted to use my CareCredit account at a dental appointment and was unexpectedly informed that my account had been closed. This came as a complete surprise, as I have been a long-standing customer who consistently maintains excellent payment behavior. I have never made a late payment, have always paid my balances on time, and in many cases, have paid balances in full without incurring interest. 

At the time of closure : CareCredit account had an approximate balance of {$440.00} with a credit limit around {$15000.00} and was being paid down through scheduled autopay toward a promotional balance. 
XXXX account had an approximate balance of {$1700.00} with a credit limit around {$7000.00}, with no missed or late payments. 
XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX account had no balance for nearly a year and a credit limit around {$7000.00}. 

After discovering the closures, I contacted Synchrony Bank and was told the accounts were closed due to recent activity suggesting potential def

I have never missed a payment on my Best Buy account. The creditor closed my account due to a score drop and retroactively charged interest on balances already paid. This has doubled my debt and made it impossible to pay off the principal. They are also charging another monthly fee on top of the monthly fee I am paying and the balance has not bugged in the past year in a half since it was closed by the company. When this happened I owed {$1500.00} and it went to {$3000.00}. I have remained paying this account at {$100.00} a month and they are charging me another {$100.00} fee a month and the total has not bugged in years. I have called several times and no one will talk to me due to the account being closed which they closed.

I am filing this complaint against Synchrony Bank / CareCredit regarding the sudden, unexplained closure of my account and Synchrony 's failure to provide required notice.

FACTS : I have held a CareCredit card issued by Synchrony Bank for approximately 10 years. During that time, I have never missed a payment, never been late, and have been an ideal customer in every respect. This account is one of my longest-standing open credit accounts.

After carrying a balance for years and diligently paying it down, my account reached a {$0.00} balance within the past year.

On or around XX/XX/year>, Synchrony closed my account without any prior notice - no letter, no email, no phone call of any kind. I only discovered the closure when I received a credit score alert notifying me that my score had dropped XXXX points as a direct result of the account closure. 

I contacted Synchrony on XX/XX/year> to understand why my account was closed and to request reinstatement. The customer service representative I spoke with was entirely unhelpful, apathetic & offered no explanation for the closure, and stated there was nothing that could be done.

assessment.why_concerning: HIGH severity: 53 consumers affected by the same pattern at SYNCHRONY FINANCIAL and others, and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated system, glitch.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 53 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated account-closure / fraud model appears to be exiting customers and reversing payments without manual review or notice.
hypotheses.evidence: 53 complaints showing automated closure and payment-reversal cues; concentrated at SYNCHRONY FINANCIAL, CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORPORATION, CITIBANK, N.A..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to SYNCHRONY FINANCIAL for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask SYNCHRONY FINANCIAL directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

I used the application to pay XXXX separate hair stylists and for the rewards program for the coffee shop I use to frequent. I never had an issue until earlier this year in XX/XX/year>, where CashApp locked my account then presented a message stating that I have illegally used my account for XXXX  activity. I initially opened a support case with CashApp, XXXX, in XX/XX/year>, to review reopening my account explaining how I have been leveraging the account and was informed that it was documented that I was using my account for XXXX XXXX activity. I recently noticed my support case was closed out in XX/XX/year>. After realizing my support case was closed, I attempted to talk to support through the chat service on XX/XX/year> and was told to contact the phone support since they couldn't provide details surrounding why my account was flagged for illegal activity on the chat service because XXXX of the federal agencies prevents the chat support from providing case related details online. So for my next steps, I called CashApp on XX/XX/year>, in attempts to receive specific details surrounding what specific actions I took that violated the TOS with them specifying which sections of the TOS covers those activities. I was unable to get anyone over chat, phone, or email to explain what I did wrong and they continue to provide the same canned response over and over again. I have provided screenshots of some of the emails from the support case where they provided absolutely no explanation and just linked me to the TOS FAQ that still doesn't explain why my account was closed. There is no explanation in the email saying even which section of the FAQ link to review to determine why my account was closed. I would like to have my account reopened because I know for a fact that I wasn't doing anything illegal, I was literally sending cash to someone. Outside of this agency, I did file a complaint though the XXXX and was me

On XX/XX/year>, I bought {$280.00} worth of bitcoin bitcoin on the app, XXXX. XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, has changed the ability to withdrawal bitcoin, and their reviews have changed with multiple complaints that they are putting security holds under false pretenses to steal money, closing accounts to steal money, and essentially scamming people out of their money in ways that ensure they are able to keep peoples money in elaborate ways and use elaborate strategies to ensure more fees and more money. After the money {$280.00} had turned into bitcoin on XX/XX/year>, they placed a security hold on my account immediately. Nothing changed that caused that hold. They use XXXX  to create automated messages when messaged about that, and they request a picture with ID, and a handwritten note with a ticket number and the date. I did that on 4 separate occasions. They closed my account, and made it so I had to sell the bitcoin with stipulations that ensure they get more money, since they will not let you withdrawal crypto after closing the account. This is all an elaborate way to steal money. They now have froze it, asked for bank information, and have automated messages that dont pertain to what they are doing. It is illegal to lock my money in the account and not allow me to withdrawal after closing. This is after proving my identity on 4 separate XXXX automated messages. They are now notorious for putting security holds on peoples money right when it turns into bitcoin, and they recently made it so no one can withdrawal their crypto without waiting at least two days. They have closed my account without a way of letting me withdrawal my money.

We are a small nonprofit that uses Venmo occasionally for customers or donors who don't have easy access to other means of payment. Venmo has locked our account from accessing funds while it requests identity documents. We have uploaded 2 separate sets of identity documents 4 times : clearly lit, clear photo, clear name and date of birth. Each time Venmo rejects them and will not tell us what's wrong with these documents, only emails us from an " unmonitored '' email inbox. 

When we ask to speak to support : - Venmo 's " Chat With Us '' link sends us to an unhelpful FAQ + an AI chatbot that is unable to provide answers - Venmo 's support line, a phone number, put us on hold for 20 minutes and then abruptly hung up, forcing us to start over. 
- We tried again, and this time chose the " callback '' option with their support line, but Venmo never called us back. 
- XXXXPaypal seems set up in every possible way to avoid letting customers speak to a human and get meaningful feedback or help instead of automated emails or useless AI chatbots. 

We just want access to our account!

case_records.product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
case_records.narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, I received a call from a phone number that displayed as Chase Bank ( XXXX ). The caller impersonated Chases fraud department and told me there was a pending XXXX transaction that would overdraw my account unless I acted immediately. The scammer used Chase-specific language, procedures, and urgency. I believed I was speaking with an actual Chase representative because the incoming number was Chases official number.

Under false pretenses, I followed the instructions they gave me because I was told it was the only way to stop the fraudulent pending transfer. I did not knowingly or willingly authorize any payment to the scammer. The scammer manipulated me into believing they were Chase and that the activity they described was legitimate fraud prevention.

Immediately after the call ended, I realized something was wrong and called Chase the very same day.

During the first call, I was having a severe panic attackshaking, crying, distressed, and overwhelmedwhich is clearly audible in the call recording. Because of this, I misspoke and said I had not spoken to anyone.To clarify, I mentally thought to myself " I wasn't actually speaking to Chase '' ; not realizing I left r

case_records.narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, I am submitting this formal complaint against Venmo ( a PayPal service ) for improperly restricting my account and refusing to allow me access to funds that were validly paid to me, without clear justification, adequate verification procedures, or timely resolution. 

Company Information Company : XXXX XXXX PayPal , XXXX XXXX 
Product/Service : XXXX wallet / peer-to-peer payment services Description of the Issue Venmo accepted a payment made XXXX XXXX, and the funds were successfully credited to my Venmo account. assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 76 consumers affected by the same pattern at Paypal Holdings, Inc and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 76 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated AML / sanctions screening is likely freezing withdrawals; a recent model change may have increased false positives on legitimate users.
hypotheses.evidence: 76 complaints showing withdrawal-freeze and verification-failure cues; concentrated at Paypal Holdings, Inc, Block, Inc., Coinbase, Inc..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Paypal Holdings, Inc for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Paypal Holdings, Inc directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Cards & Payments — Closure, American, Rewards — Closing your account
sample_narratives: I had a problem with an expensive transaction from XXXX that went on for MONTHS. I had to get Wells Fargo involved to try and facilitate communication since XXXX XXXX was not getting back to me. A MONTH after opening the dispute ( $ XXXX ) with Wells Fargo I learned they had done absolutely nothing. They didn't reach out to XXXX, they hadn't even documented the information I gave them on day one. Instead, they kept sending me information requests and I had learned they put the dispute into a " information needed queue '' which was going nowhere. So, I filed a CFPB complaint for them no doing anything XXXX and got a phone call saying " we're going to start working on it '' .... weeks went by and I ( without any bit of help from Wells Fargo ) was able to finally get my money back from XXXX Hardware. NOT to mention that this entire time XXXX was trying to issue $ XXXX in refunds to gift cards to me instead of the original payment method - which I did inform WFC of. About a week after the transaction was returned to my card ( which WFC can obviously see ) they gave me a " temporary credit '' of {$5600.00} to my card. Like XXXX? They're clearly not doing their jobs and not paying attention to anything that happens regarding this. Even now - DAYS after I've called and reported this to them - the adjustment remains and directly contradicts my daily balance keeping ( because I do not trust Wells Fargo to report activity accurately ).

Chase sent me an automated fraud alert for an unfamiliar {$5.00} charge. I responded No, expecting the bank to verify the transaction. Instead, Chase immediately shut down my debit card without providing any notice that the card was being deactivated. I learned of the shutdown only after multiple legitimate recurring payments began failing.

Why this is a violation : Chases actions appear to violate several consumer-protection standards : Regulation E ( 12 CFR 1005 ) requires that banks provide timely notice when restricting access to a consumers debit card or electronic fund transfers. Chase did not notify me in any way that the card was being closed.

This is potentially an unfair practice under UDAAP because Chases automated decision caused significant and predictable harm ( failed payments, fees, service interruptions ) while offering no reasonable consumer benefit and providing no opportunity for me to correct or verify the alert.

The card closure was disproportionate to the situationone low-dollar charge with no fraud patternshowing a systemically unreasonable fraud-workflow design that harms consumers.

No adverse action notice was provided, despite restricting my access to an active payment instrument linked to multiple accounts.

Chase failed to follow reasonable verification procedures before taking action that impacted every account linked to that debit card.

Resulting damages : Because Chase closed the card without notice, several legitimate payments were declined, causing financial loss, service interruptions, and the risk of negative credit reporting. Chases conduct deprived me of access to an active and relied-upon payment method with no opportunity to prevent the harm.

What I am requesting : The CFPB should escalate this matter to Chase and require the bank to : Provide a written explanation of their justification for closing a debit card without any notice after a routine fraud-alert response.

Explain how this practice complies with Regulation E

On XX/XX/XXXX, I used my American Express card through XXXX XXXX  to purchase a vinyl record from XXXX ( amount {$34.00} ). The order confirmation stated : Estimated delivery : up to XXXX weeks and XXXX XXXX. 

The merchant repeatedly told me throughout XXXX that the order was delayed but still would ship. I have an email from XX/XX/XXXX confirming the delay and instructing me to continue waiting. Based on the merchants ongoing assurances, I did not dispute the charge earlier. 

The product has never been delivered. The merchant continues to advertise the item as Backordered XXXX weeks, demonstrating ongoing misrepresentation of availability. 

When I contacted American Express to dispute the charge for non-receipt, they refused, incorrectly citing the XXXX billing-error rule under Regulation Z. This is not a billing error ; it is a failure to deliver goods, which is covered by card network dispute rules allowing exceptions where merchants delay delivery to avoid chargebacks. 

A supervisor opened a dispute manually on XX/XX/XXXX, but American Express automatically closed it within minutes, without reviewing my documents or allowing me to upload proof. 

I am asking the CFPB to require American Express to intervene appropriately for a clear failure-to-deliver case and issue a refund.

Complaint : I am filing a complaint regarding the unexplained closure of my XXXX credit card account. I opened the account recently, paid my first bill in full, and paid my second bill off immediately. My payment history was perfect, and my balance was {$0.00} at the time the account was closed. 

Despite this, XXXX closed my account without any notice, explanation, or request for verification. After the closure, my account showed a full available credit limit, but I was unable to use the card. My available credit displayed as {$0.00} even though the account had no balance. 

Since the closure, XXXX  has continued sending me statements and issuing refunds to this closed account. This is confusing and inconsistent, as it suggests the account is still being treated as active for some purposes while being restricted for others. 

I am requesting : - A clear explanation for why my account was closed - Confirmation that this closure will not negatively affect my credit report - Clarification on why statements and refunds are still

case_records.narrative: On XX/XX/year>, my XXXX SkyMiles Platinum American Express credit card account was charged an annual membership fee of {$300.00}. I did not cancel the account. On XX/XX/year>, when I accessed my American Express app, I noticed that my card had been cancelled without prior notice. I was later informed that the account was flagged for alleged fraud, but no specific transactions or details were provided. On the same day, I contacted American Express and spent approximately 60 minutes on the phone seeking an explanation, documentation, or identification of the charges that led to the fraud determination. During this call, American Express was unable to provide any explanation or supporting information. As a result of the account closure, I was unable to use the benefits associated with the annual fee, and XXXX SkyMiles were forfeited without explanation or opportunity to redeem them. I requested a refund or prorated refund of the annual fee and clarification or reinstatement of my SkyMiles, but American Express refused or was unable to provide a resolution despite my efforts to resolve the matter directly.

case_records.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
case_records.issue: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
case_records.narrative:
On Tuesday, XX/XX/year>, I contacted Wells Fargo immediately to report and freeze {$9200.00} in pending fraudulent XXXX XXXX XXXX ' transactions. The frontline representative closed the card ending in XXXX but failed to log the dispute correctly. 

Due to an internal Wells Fargo software mapping error, the bank allowed the explicit {$9200.00} in fraudulent charges to seamlessly transfer over and permanently post onto my newly issued credit card number rather than blocking them. Because their automated system checked the old card number and saw that the charges hadn't finalized on that specific line, it erroneously generated a letter dated XX/XX/year> ( Claim ID : XXXX ), stating the transactions 'did not post ' and falsely claiming all posted charges on my account were valid and authorized. 

When I called to correct this administrative error, the bank refused to issue an immediate provisional credit. Instead, they forced me to issue a third credit card number, start a brand-new dispute file, and wait an additional 7 to 10 business days while holding a {$9200.00} fraudulent balance against me on Wells Fargo Case Number XXXX.

assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 73 consumers affected by the same pattern at AMERICAN EXPRESS COMPANY and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated loop, automated system.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 73 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated account-closure / fraud model appears to be exiting customers and reversing payments without manual review or notice.
hypotheses.evidence: 73 complaints showing automated closure and payment-reversal cues; concentrated at AMERICAN EXPRESS COMPANY, CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORPORATION, WELLS FARGO & COMPANY.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to AMERICAN EXPRESS COMPANY for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask AMERICAN EXPRESS COMPANY directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes
