assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 56 consumers affected by the same pattern at Block, Inc. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated system, chatbot, computer error.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 56 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated AML / sanctions screening is likely freezing withdrawals; a recent model change may have increased false positives on legitimate users.
hypotheses.evidence: 56 complaints showing withdrawal-freeze and verification-failure cues; concentrated at Block, Inc., Paypal Holdings, Inc, WELLS FARGO & COMPANY.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Block, Inc. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Block, Inc. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

clusters.name: Banking — Check, Deposit, Funds — Managing an account
sample_narratives:
Summary : USAA Federal Savings Bank incorrectly altered the amount of a mobile check deposit from {$1500.00} to {$15.00} three days after the funds were credited to my account. USAAs automated system made this change without human review, without notifying me, and deducted {$1400.00} from my account. When contacted about the error, the representative confirmed that the check images in their system clearly stated {$1500.00} but also stated that there was absolutely no way to correct the error and that the only way for me to get the money back was to ask the original check issuer to write me a new check for the difference. I escalated the request to their executive branch, and I received an email reiterating the original representative 's statements. 

Issue : The original check is clearly written for {$1500.00}. Despite acknowledging the error, USAA representatives and supervisors stated they can not correct the error and that only the check issuer can remedy the situation. This is an improper denial of responsibility for an entirely internal banking error. 

Impact : This caused my account balance to be incorrect without notification or consent.

I opened a checking account with Capital One Checking on XX/XX/XXXX and deposited my work check in the amount of {$1000.00} and it said it would be available the following Wednesday, XX/XX/XXXX. I looked at my account on the XXXX and it still wasnt showing available balance. I called them and they said that the amount wouldnt be available until the XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX due to them not being able to verify the phone number of my employer. They got ahold of my employer and everything by was verified and then they said it would clear in the next 10 days. So I looked at my account today, the XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX and it was still showing XXXX available balance. I called them again on the XXXX and they told me they were holding the check until XX/XX/XXXX because there was no signature on the check from me. I signed the check! And shouldnt they have reached out to me or reversed the check if I didnt sign the check? I have been getting the run around from them! They have my money but wont release it to me. They keep changing the reasons of why they are holding it.

First I'd like to complain about how difficult it is to resolve anything with Capital One as you have only the option to call. They froze my account for no reason, and I was offered no explanation. I was able to resolve this after self ID-ing 6 times, 3.5 hours of phone calls and embarrassingly having to have a 3 way call with my old bank- so they could say the same thing I had been telling Capital One. They unfroze my account but I then had to call back to get access to online banking. Call again to get acess to my debit card. Etc I felt like a child in trouble with their divorced parents. My complaint now is I mobile deposited a portion of my payroll check and they will not be releasing the funds for 6 business days which will be a total of 8 days. I find this absurd and potentially illegal as I was under the impression {$270.00} must be released the next business day, it is the same company that direct deposits into the account, and it is a check issued by the state in which I live- as I am a XXXX XXXX. My main ( XXXX XXXX ) Direct Deposit payroll is also sent by the state on Wednesdays but Capital One will not release funds until Friday - when other banks would issue the funds the day after it is sent.

I find Capital One is intentionally making it difficult for me to bank with them and would like them to stop making it difficult for me to access payroll funds, and my account in general. I work odd hours this time of year and making a phone call is nearly impossible - I have had approximately 4 hours home since XX/XX/year>.

On Monday, XX/XX/XXXX, I deposited a legitimate government unemployment refund check into my E * TRADE account. The funds were made available immediately. 

On Tuesday, XX/XX/XXXX, my account was suddenly frozen without prior notice, and my debit card was declined at a restaurant. When I contacted customer service, I was questioned about whether I had written the check to myself.

assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 26 consumers affected by the same pattern at BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION and others, and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, artificial intelligence, automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 26 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: The firm likely tightened an automated transaction-monitoring / financial-crime model, raising false-positive exit rates — legitimate customers are 'de-risked' and offboarded without manual review.
hypotheses.evidence: 26 complaints showing account-closure and suspicious-activity language concentrated at a few firms; concentrated at BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORPORATION, JPMORGAN CHASE & CO..

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated fraud-detection model may be flagging legitimate transactions, freezing funds before any human check.
hypotheses.evidence: 26 complaints showing fund-freeze and access-loss cues; concentrated at BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORPORATION, JPMORGAN CHASE & CO..

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

clusters.name: Cards & Payments — Citi, Payments, Fraud — Closing your account
sample_narratives: Upon 1 hour and 5 attempts through the voice bot and chat bot this company has a really tough process to close their AA citi advantage card. My call was abandoned 3 times in the IVR once I did get a rep offshore they were talking over me claiming they need to see if they can even cancel my card. The rep then went into benefits and ignored the four direct requests I made to " close the account '' I was then put on hold and the call was abandoned again. This is not a good way to treat people. I don't have time to get the runaround or try and get through a system gained to make it very difficult to cancel a credit card.

I applied for the Citi / AAdvantage Platinum Select Card on Wednesday, XX/XX/year>. Citi placed the application under review for identity verification due to a fraud alert on my credit report but did not request ID verification until I manually called in to check on the status of my application on Saturday, XX/XX/year>. Citi attempted outbound phone calls for identity verification, but the calls repeatedly failed to go through. 

Because the calls did not go through, the application was automatically denied for being unable to verify identity. I repeatedly contacted Citi by phone and visited multiple Citi branches, but was told that no further action could be taken. One Citi phone rep told me that they could only verify by phone using the same method ( which repeatedly failed ) despite me knowing someone else who recently applied to the same exact card and also had a fraud alert on file and had their ID verified through text message using their phone number on file with the fraud alert.

This denial was not based on creditworthiness. I have an excellent credit profile, existing Citi credit cards, and the application was denied solely due to failed outbound verification calls. The Citi reps I spoke with before told me that they could not even run the application until my ID was verified via phone call, so it appears that it the application was not even fully run. The application status immediately flipped to denial after the 5th failed ID verification call on Sunday, XX/XX/year>. I am requesting that Citi reopen or reevaluate the application and provide a reasonable method to complete identity verification ( e.g., in-person verification in a branch, text message, or other legitimate alternative method ).

I received a fraud transaction on my Citi Bank Visa. I contacted Citi Bank which they are doing nothing. They are giving me a run around to dispute & remove this fraud charge. 
They give me other phone numbers to call which I am put on hold & my call is never picked up. 
When I call to make my credit card bank aware of a fraud/scam charge, I expect my credit card company to take care of this, Citi Bank is not providing me this service.

I realizsed the booking error immediately and filed the dispute with Citi as soon as the charge appeared. I remained in constant communication with Citi throughout the entire process, responding promptly to every request and following up repeatedly. Despite this, Citi allowed the payment to proceed and settle while the dispute was still active.

assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 23 consumers affected by the same pattern at CITIBANK, N.A., and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 23 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated account-closure / fraud model appears to be exiting customers and reversing payments without manual review or notice.
hypotheses.evidence: 23 complaints showing automated closure and payment-reversal cues; concentrated at CITIBANK, N.A..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to CITIBANK, N.A. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask CITIBANK, N.A. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Payments & Crypto — Paypal, Automated, Transaction — Trouble accessing funds in your mobile or digital wallet
sample_narratives:
In XX/XX/year>, approximately {$900.00} in unauthorized subscription charges were made through my PayPal account. These charges were created using an email address that had been hacked. I did not authorize these subscriptions or any related purchases. I never agreed to any recurring payments, and I did not knowingly create or approve any subscription agreement. 

I opened multiple disputes with PayPal starting XX/XX/year> when I called and had the agent force stop the subscription and I changed my email address and password to my PayPal account to protect it from further transactions. I was informed to contact the seller through the PayPal dispute page, which I did on XX/XX/XXXX, with no response. I have called PayPal on XXXX XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX speaking to agents who all verify that the activity is fraudulent and to expect a full refund after their 10 day investigation period. Each time, the cases were automatically closed in favor of the seller without a proper investigation. PayPal repeatedly stated that I had an agreement for the subscription, but I could not have created this agreement because the email account associated with the activity was compromised and not under my control. 

I contacted PayPal by phone several times and attempted to escalate the issue as an Unauthorized Account Access case. I asked for details such as proof of authorization, device/IP information, and a manual review by the Account Compromise Investigations team . PayPal did not provide this information and continued sending automated responses that did not address my situation. 

PayPal has required that I resolve the issue with the merchant first, but the merchant ( XXXX ) will not allow me to access the account because the subscription was created under the hacked email address that I do not control. However, I emailed their dispute team on XX/XX/year> with documentation of the charges and have no received a response. 

This situation has been ongo

I am filing a complaint against PayPal regarding the sudden and unexplained permanent deactivation of my long-standing personal account and the holding of potential funds for up to 120 days. 

I have been a PayPal customer since approximately XXXX. This is a personal account only. I do not operate a business through this account. I have not engaged in high-risk transactions, international transactions, large volume transfers, cryptocurrency, gambling, adult content, policy-restricted activity, or anything remotely suspicious. To my knowledge, I have never had chargebacks, disputes, fraud claims, or compliance issues on this account. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a notice that my account was permanently deactivated due to unusual activity with no specifics provided and no opportunity to correct or clarify anything. The email states funds may be held for up to 120 days. When I contacted customer support, I was told I could receive no further information unless I wrote a physical letter to their legal department.

I believe this is unfair, lacks transparency, and may violate consumer protection principles. A financial institution should not be allowed to permanently ban a consumer and restrict access to their financial platform without : explaining the specific reason citing the exact policy allegedly violated offering meaningful appeal or review I am requesting that PayPal : -Provide a detailed written explanation of the specific activity or policy that led to the permanent deactivation of my account. 
-Conduct a full manual review of my account, given it is a long-standing personal account with normal activity. 
-Confirm whether any funds are being held and ensure they are released as soon as legally permissible. 
-Reinstate my account if no legitimate compliance violation exists. 

This situation has created unnecessary stress, uncertainty, and disruption. I simply want transparency and fair handling. 


Thank you!

PayPal permanently limited my business account and placed a XXXX hold on my funds without providing a clear or detailed explanation. I had been using my PayPal Business account for legitimate small-business payments for XXXX years with no issues. 

When I started receiving payments for my business, PayPal suddenly closed my account and claimed it was due to a previous account with unresolved issues from years ago. I have not been given any documentation, details, or proof of what the supposed issue is. Customer service told me the decision is final and that no one at PayPal can assist me further. 

I have requested clarification and documentation, but PayPal has refused to explain what data, accounts, or risk information they used to justify permanently limiting my account and holding my money. This lack of transparency is affecting my business income and violates my right to understand the basis of a financial decision made against me. 

I am submitting this complaint because PayPal has not provided clear reasoning or supporting evidence for limiting my account and withholding my funds.

case_records.product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
case_records.issue: Money was not available when promised
case_records.narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, my business processed a single transaction of {$2000.00} for the final delivery of a custom-built software application. Shortly after, I received an automated email from PayPal informing me that my account had been permanently deactivated. The email explicitly stated my account was placed in the 'High Tier ' and subject to a 'High Volume Dispute fee ' due to customer dissatisfaction.

However, this stated reason is mathematically impossible and factually incorrect. My account has exa

ctly one transaction in its entire history, and there are absolutely zero disputes, claims, or chargebacks associated with it.

I contacted PayPal customer service by phone to appeal this algorithmic error. Still, the representative informed me that the automated system had locked their interface and that they lacked authorization to request a manual review or escalate the issue. As a result, PayPal is currently holding my {$2000.00} balance for up to 120 days on the basis of a completely false premise. The system has misidentified a single, fully accepted, and undisputed XXXX software transaction as a high-volume dispute risk, and the company has completely blocked standard customer s

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 53 consumers affected by the same pattern at Paypal Holdings, Inc, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated loop, automated system.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 53 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated AML / sanctions screening is likely freezing withdrawals; a recent model change may have increased false positives on legitimate users.
hypotheses.evidence: 53 complaints showing withdrawal-freeze and verification-failure cues; concentrated at Paypal Holdings, Inc.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Paypal Holdings, Inc for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Paypal Holdings, Inc directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

clusters.name: Banking — Wells, Fargo, Wells Fargo — Managing an account
sample_narratives:
I called Wells Fargo to see what is going on with my card as it hadnt been delivered and had been past 5-7 buisness days. I explicitly told a Wells Fargo agent not to cancel or replace my debit card. Despite this, the agent processed a replacement, which automatically canceled the valid card I later received the same day. I was unable to activate it. I was unaware of this until I tried activating it and was having problems and had to call bank and was told card was closed.

When I called back to request a manager, the manager disconnected the call and never tried calling me back.This cancellation was not authorized, caused delays in accessing my funds, and required additional calls. I am requesting expedited replacement, documentation of agent error, and assurance this does not happen again.

On recorded call, I at no point authorized or consented for replacement to cancel my card and issue a replacement. I even clarified to agent that I did not want him to try to issue a replacement. I have bills that need to be paid and have been waiting for 2 weeks for this card only for it to be cancelled without my authorization which is a serious violation of banking rules and my customer rights. This is improper and unauthorized behavior by this Wells Fargo agent. This agent ignored my instructions and thats unauthorized action by this employee and a breach of internal bank policy.

I was told I now have to wait till Monday to call again because there department is closed till Monday.

On XX/XX/XXXXI deposited a check wth the amount of XXXX from my husband via atm. They help help the check until XXXX XXXX due to concerns with signature. Once I called his bank I was told Wells Fargo has already taken the money from his account. I called back. No one could explain to me how the money had been drafted to his account but not put into mine. Nor what was wrong with the signature except it was suspicious. Oh my husbands also on some of my Wells Fargo accounts. I also had to request a new debit card due to fraudulent charges. It says it was delivered on the XXXX but its now the XXXX and its no where to be found. I called again explaining all my auto draft bill would be coming through because it was the first. XXXX Wells Fargo employee told me it was just unfortunate and an other said she was submitting a claim. I received a claim number but no email no returned call no nothing. Its like it was never submitted. Now here I am with no way to pay and no card number to input on my bills resulting in late fees. Can someone please help me understand how this all happened and why am I being held responsible?

I am sick and tired of this runaround and closing of my cases because no one is doing their job/position correctly. The CFPB is acting like a child that does not understand it's own power, and the XXXX bank Wells Fargo is continuously acting in a fraudulent manner. I demand my case be reopened and properly administrated because someone knows what is going on. My case and responses from the XXXX XXXX  have been improperly administered. I demand a proper attendant be placed on my account to administer positive results to me, otherwise you and everyone else who is behaving in this manner are accessories to the hindrance of my constitutional rights. My address is XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, Maryland XXXX. Someone is letting the computer do the human work that is necessary to end this nightmare. Someone is taking the easy way out and performing evil illegal unconstitutional acts on a XXXX XXXX. I demand justice. If need be, I want to perform a XXXXXXXX XXXX arrest. Who is in charge of this agency? I read that agency employees will get paid so there is no reason to hinder my case. What could be the issue here? I am not playing games because I will sue Wells Fargo. I should have had access to procedures for legal action against Wells Fargo. You think who I am is a joke. I really am who I say I am, but I believe there is something terribly terribly wrong here with the credentials of those administering my case at the CFPB. I am not a XXXX  person, but I really do feel very very XXXX  right here and now. These illegal activities are taking place because someone at the CFPB IS NOT DOING THEIR JOB. THE COMPANY 'S FEEDBACK IS A REFLECTION OF WHAT YOUR JOB SHOULD BE FILLING. YOU ARE ALMOST COMPLETELY ROBOTIC and act as if you are not here to serve the public. Talking about FEEDBACK ONLY I RECEIVED FROM THE COMPANY, THIS IS IT WHICH I HAVE FORMERLY STATED TO YOU BECAUSE YOU ARE A REFLECTION OF EVERYTHING WELLS FARGO IS FALSELY STATING TO ME. YOU HAVE BEEN DISRESPECTFUL, UNPROFESSIONAL

case_records.issue: Problem caused by your funds being low
case_records.narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, at approximately XXXX a.m., I contacted Wells Fargo customer service regarding XXXX overdraft fees that were assessed on my account over the past XXXX weeks. 

My account is explicitly set to reject transactions when insufficient funds are available. Despite this setting, some transactions were rejected while others were allowed to post, resulting in overdraft fees. This inconsistent handling directly contradicts my account preferences and the way my previous Wells Fargo account functioned for years. 

During the call, the initial representative refunded XXXX of the XXXX overdraft fees. I requested escalation to discuss why transactions were inconsistently approved and to request reversal of the third fee. 

I was then transferred to a supervisor named XXXX. At first, he engaged normally, but once I questioned the inconsistency and requested the remaining overdraft fee reversal, his demeanor became robotic, dismissive, and condescending. 

The supervisor initially stated that the overdraft occurred because the transaction was a recurring payment. I explained that other recurring payments during the same period were rejected due to insufficient funds, consistent with

Company : Wells Fargo Bank Issue Type : Checking or savings account Problem : Problem with a deposit or withdrawal Sub-issue : ATM deposit not credited On XX/XX/XXXX, I deposited cash into a Wells Fargo ATM. The ATM accepted the deposit ; however, {$100.00} of that deposit was never credited to my account. 
I promptly filed a claim to avoid any delay in reporting. Wells Fargo later denied my claim based solely on their statement that the ATM audit did not reveal any loose or jammed funds. 

I respectfully dispute this conclusion. The absence of loose cash during an audit does not conclusively prove that the ATM did not malfunction or temporarily retain the funds. ATMs are complex mechanical systems, and plausible explanations include delayed mechanical release, internal retention errors, delayed expulsion of funds after my departure, or reconciliation discrepancies. 
Over the past two months, I have made multiple calls attempting to resolve this issue. On XX/XX/XXXX, I spent approximately one and a half hours speaking with representatives across several departments, repeatedly requesting escalation and reinvestigation. 

Also on XX/XX/XXXX, I sent a formal written complaint to Well

I am extremely frustrated and disappointed with the way my situation has been handled by Wells Fargo. I trusted this bank to properly manage my account and protect my money, but instead I have been put through weeks of stress, confusion, and inconvenience because of the way this issue was handled. 

The situation began when Wells Fargo allowed my rent payment to be charged twice from my account. This was not a small mistake. Rent is a large and important payment, and having it taken out twice created serious financial strain for me. As soon as I noticed the duplicate charge, I immediately contacted Wells Fargo to have the issue corrected. I specifically requested that a stop payment be placed so the duplicate charge would not go through. Instead of properly following that request, the bank chose to open a claim on the transaction instead. 

During this process, I spoke with multiple representatives who repeatedly assured me that my account was fine and that everything had been handled correctly. Based on what I was told by Wells Fargo staff, I believed the issue had been resolved and that my account was in good standing. I relied on the information your employees gave me. 

Then, t

Wells Fargo has decided to close my account because we use XXXX too many times. They dont publish a limit and my wife was using it together money for a destination wedding, and all they said is that its being used too much for a personal account. That is not illegal or fraudulent. 

Fine they can close the account, but they have issued a block and will not let us access our money and they are not following their own policies. I have received XXXX conflicting letters with completely different information from them. 

XX/XX/XXXX I received a letter stating they are closing the account as of XX/XX/XXXX and that all funds are being held and blocked. 

XX/XX/XXXX I received another letter staining their closing the account as of XX/XX/XXXX and I WILL HAVE access to all of the funds until it is closed. 

This is deceptive, inconsistent, and unethical, and Wells Fargo should be ashamed of themselves for letting a computer algorithm incorrectly hold peoples money. 

I called customer service about these conflicting letters and received no assistance

Nevertheless, We

I have an everyday checking account at Wells Fargo. My atm debit withdrawal limit is {$1000.00}. I called Wells Fargo to increase my withdrawal limit to {$2000.00}. The representative said he has to enter it into the system and see what it says. The limit increase was denied. I work in financial services and understand the risk of increasing atm withdrawal limits and that it's for the protection of the customer. I know all too well about how it's for fraud protection. If it's my funds in the account, I should be able to request the increase. I plan to close out this account, however, I don't feel comfortable parking my new car outside a branch with the possibility of someone scratching my car or sideswiping my car. Then going inside to wait in a long line to withdrawal all the funds and then be told to see a banker to process the closure of the account. Banking is supposed to be convenient for the customer. I just wanted to withdrawal through the atm and then call customer service to close out the account. It's been a long time since I've had to step foot into a branch since you can do a lot digitally. Just many rude, condescending bankers who speak in circles and give no real answers. 
I can provide names and dates if needed.

his will cause an immediate and severe financial hardship, leaving me unable to provide food, shelter, and medical needs for my child. Please instruct the bank to freeze the reversal pending the outcome.

I tried to close my bank account on XX/XX/year> for the first time and then I tried to close it multiple times after that with the last attempt being XX/XX/XXXX or XXXX. I have records of these calls where I attempted to cancel my account and the automated system would say thank you, goodbye any time I said I want to close my account as the reason for the call. On XX/XX/XXXX or XXXX I finally got the customer service rep on the phone and I made sure to have her put that Im trying to close my account as a note in my account. She did that. Wells Fargo has confirmed to me that she did that but they are still trying to make me pay overdraft fees and service fees for the time period after I tried to close the account. I should not be held responsible for any fees incurred following my very first time calling to close the account. Wells Fargo is notorious for being difficult to close accounts with.

Date : XX/XX/year> To Whom It May Concern, I am requesting a federal investigation into Wells Fargo for mishandled fraud claims, failure to protect a compromised account, failure to intervene during an obvious scam, branch negligence, privacy violations, and repeated Regulation E violations. My total loss was {$18000.00}, plus additional credit card fraud that Wells Fargo refused to reverse. The banks actions and inaction caused severe financial and emotional harm that continues today.

clusters.name: Banking — Chase, Business, Accounts — Managing an account
sample_narratives:
Company : JPMorgan Chase Bank , N.A . ( Chase ) What happened : On XX/XX/year>, counterfeit/duplicate checks posted against our Chase checking account. We wrote one legitimate check for {$200.00}, but two separate counterfeit checks posted for {$600.00} each ( total {$1200.00} ). These items were not authorized by us. 

The day before the {$600.00} checks posted ( XX/XX/year> ), there was a separate counterfeit check attempt for {$2000.00}. We contacted Chases fraud/claims department immediately. The agent told us it was only an algorithm/system error and reversed it as a glitch rather than treating it as check fraud. Because it was misclassified and not investigated or contained ( e.g., no check block/ no urgent account protection ), the criminals were able to try again and two counterfeit {$600.00} checks posted the next day.

Compounding this : our Chase debit card states call for XXXX assistance, but when we attempted to call during the overnight period to prevent posting, the phone lines were closed. We also used the Chase chatbot, which directed us to call multiple numbers presented as XXXX assistance ; those numbers were also closed. This prevented us from giving Chase a timely heads-up before the fraudulent items posted and caused additional harm. 

Actions taken / Chase response : We spoke with an in-branch banker, three phone agents, and finally a supervisor.

The branch helped open a new account and file disputes, but Chase stated it would take XXXX hours for XXXX item and XXXX business days for another to reverse, leaving us without funds around a major holiday. 

We requested immediate provisional credit because these are unauthorized counterfeit items. The supervisor refused, stating Chase does not provide provisional credit for check fraud. 

We also requested documentation needed for law enforcement ( copies/images of the checks front/back, claim/case documentation, and deposit/processing details ). Chase refused to provide meaningful assistance for

On XX/XX/year>, i attempted to open a savings account with OpenBank ( santander ). I was declined. I called to find out reason for the decline and was told there is no reason. I informed that they have to provide a reason and was told they do not need to provide a reason. I requested a call back from a supervisor and no one returned my call.

I opened my business checking account back on XX/XX/XXXX with no issue at all online. I opened it under a new account promotion bonus offer which was {$400.00} for maintaing a {$2000.00} average daily balance for XXXX full XXXX cycles. I brought my balance up to above {$2000.00} right away, and used the account for normal purchases after receiving my debit card. 3 months go by with no issue at all, and on XX/XX/XXXX Iog into my account, and see my entire balance pending withdrawl. I immediately call customer service to see what's going on, and i'm told " my account is being closed ''. No explanation as to why, no Email or phone call beforehand. I finally get some paper in the mail XXXX XX/XX/XXXX saying my account was officially closed on XX/XX/XXXX. I'm still waiting for my entire account balance to be refunded to me, no word on how long that will take. I think it's very very suspicious how they decide to close my account with 5 days left in the final XXXX XXXX which means I would have earned my {$400.00} promotional credit award. Seems to me like this is a scam they run to use your money as they see fit until the final days left when you will earn the reward, and then decide to close out your account so they do not have to pay out the money. Such a bad look on them as a financial institution, and a very scam-like practice. I can't even go to a local branch to get my money, I have to wait on them to take their sweet time to send me out my money via a cashiers check. Horrible banking practices, and customer service is even worse.

assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 33 consumers affected by the same pattern at JPMORGAN CHASE & CO. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated system, chatbot, glitch.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 33 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: The firm likely tightened an automated transaction-monitoring / financial-crime model, raising false-positive exit rates — legitimate customers are 'de-risked' and offboarded without manual review.
hypotheses.evidence: 33 complaints showing account-closure and suspicious-activity language concentrated at a few firms; concentrated at JPMORGAN CHASE & CO., BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, CITIBANK, N.A..

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated fraud-detection model may be flagging legitimate transactions, freezing funds before any human check.
hypotheses.evidence: 33 complaints showing fund-freeze and access-loss cues; concentrated at JPMORGAN CHASE & CO., BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, CITIBANK, N.A..

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to JPMORGAN CHASE & CO. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask JPMORGAN CHASE & CO. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

case_records.narrative:
This is a serious case of Regulation E violations and UDAAP ( Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ). Under federal law ( the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ), once you notify a financial institution of an error, they are legally required to conduct a " good faith '' investigation. Hanging up on a customer mid-report and misfiling disputes to trigger automatic denials are direct violations of these protections.

Below is a structured draft for your CFPB complaint. You should copy and paste this into the " What happened? '' section of the CFPB Complaint Portal.

case_records.narrative:
My Cash App account and Cash App Card were recently deactivated. I received an email stating that my Cash App Card was deactivated and that any future paychecks sent to my account would be returned.

I have had this account for approximately three years and have only used it for normal personal transactions such as sending money to friends or helping with personal expenses.

When I contacted Cash App support through both chat and phone, I was told that my account violated their Terms of Service. However, no representative was able or willing to explain what specific action or transaction violated their policies. I was simply told that the decision was final.

The only transaction that may have been misunderstood was a payment I sent to a friend to help them pay a pawn shop loan payment. In the payment note I wrote {$100.00} for food and {$140.00} XXXX loan payment. This wording referred to helping a friend make a loan payment on an item held at a pawn shop. I was not purchasing, selling, or transferring a firearm or any restricted item. 

It is possible that this wording was misunderstood by an automated system reviewing payment notes. If so, I respectfully request that Cash App re

I also submitted an XXXX XXXX Report # [ REDACTED ]. The experience has been far has been far ideal : Failure to Investigate & Inconsistent Outcomes : Despite all transactions stemming from the same incident and evidence, Cash App randomly closed portions of the investigation without notifying me or providing a substantive explanation, while others remain open. This demonstrates that a " thorough and timely investigation '' was not performed.Failure to Provide Provisional Credit : It has been over XXXX business days since my report and a provisional credit for the {$1600.00} has not been issued.Inadequate XXXX XXXX  : My attempts to use your " XXXX live-person support '' resulted in agents closing chats prematurely without re-opening cases or ensuring the attached FBI XXXX documentation was reviewed.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 32 consumers affected by the same pattern at Block, Inc., and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 32 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated AML / sanctions screening is likely freezing withdrawals; a recent model change may have increased false positives on legitimate users.
hypotheses.evidence: 32 complaints showing withdrawal-freeze and verification-failure cues; concentrated at Block, Inc..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Block, Inc. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Block, Inc. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 38 consumers affected by the same pattern at Paypal Holdings, Inc, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 38 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated AML / sanctions screening is likely freezing withdrawals; a recent model change may have increased false positives on legitimate users.
hypotheses.evidence: 38 complaints showing withdrawal-freeze and verification-failure cues; concentrated at Paypal Holdings, Inc.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Paypal Holdings, Inc for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Paypal Holdings, Inc directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Credit Reporting — Reporting, Inaccurate, Entries — Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
sample_narratives:
I disputed several inaccurate and unverifiable accounts on my TransUnion credit report. On XX/XX/year>, TransUnion responded claiming that all disputed items were verified as accurate, yet they provided no documentation, and no explanation of how the information was verified. 
Under FCRA 611 ( a ), TransUnion is required to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation. Instead, they appear to have relied on automated electronic responses rather than obtaining actual documentation from the furnishers.

TransUnion also failed to provide the legally required Method of Verification, as required under FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 6 ) ( B ) ( iii ).

This is a violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act.

TransUnion continues to report several accounts that are : Inaccurate, Incomplete, Unverifiable, Lacking supporting documentation These accounts include ( but may not be limited to ) : XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX ) XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX ( both accounts ) XXXX XXXX These furnishers have not provided documentation proving the accounts belong to me or validating their reporting. 
The inaccurate reporting and TransUnions refusal to properly investigate has caused : Lower credit scores, denial of credit opportunities, higher interest rates, emotional distress and financial harm.

I recently received a copy of my credit report that you mailed to me with the investigation results, and I noticed that you are reporting a * * late payment for XX/XX/year> * * on my * * XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX * * account ending in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 

I HAVE NOT authorized the reporting of inaccurate or unverifiable derogatory information on my consumer file. I am writing this myself. No third party, no automated system, and no credit repair company is involved.

Your company is in clear violation of the law. Under * * 15 USC 1681e ( b ) * * MAXIMUM POSSIBLE ACCURACY you are required to ensure all information reported is accurate, complete, and verifiable. A late payment can not be reported without documented proof from the furnisher.

Under * * 15 USC 1681i * *, if information can not be verified with actual documentation, it MUST be deleted immediately.

Additionally, your reporting violates * * Metro 2 Compliance Standards * *, which your agency is required to follow : * * Metro 2 Field 17A ( Account Status ) : * * You reported the account as CURRENT.

* * Metro 2 Field 24 ( Payment Rating ) : * * You also reported a delinquency code for XX/XX/year>. 
These two fields * * CAN NOT legally coexist * * unless the delinquency is fully documented and verifiable.

No such documentation has been provided.

This is a direct violation of Metro 2 guidelines and FCRA 623 ( a ), which prohibit reporting of inaccurate or unverified derogatory data.

-- - Failure to respond satisfactorily with deletion of the above referenced inaccurate item and failure to send a free updated report showing the correction will result in legal action being taken against your company. I will also be seeking * * {$1000.00} per violation * * for : 1. Defamation of Character ( per se ) 2. Negligent Enablement of Identity Fraud 3. Fair Credit Reporting Act 15 USC 1681 violations 4. Civil liability for willful noncompliance * * 616 / 15 USC 1681n * * I have already submitted this matter to the *

Google North America Inc. ( dba Google Fi ) performed a Hard Credit Inquiry on my XXXX report on XX/XX/year> without " Permissible Purpose '' ( FCRA Section 604 ).

1. Admission of Automated/Unauthorized Pull : I was browsing a shopping cart and did not complete checkout or sign an application. In a written response to my FCC complaint ( Agent " Rio '' ), Google admitted : " if the necessary personal information... is already saved... simply moving past this step can automatically trigger a credit check. '' This confirms Google uses an automated system to execute hard credit pulls using legacy stored data without obtaining a specific, affirmative application for the new transaction.

2. Refusal to Correct ( FCRA Violation ) : I disputed this directly with Google. In a written response ( XXXX " XXXX '' ), Google stated : " Google Fi is not in a position to remove or amend a credit inquiry ... only the credit bureaus ... can investigate. '' This is false. As a Data Furnisher, Google has a legal obligation under the FCRA to correct inaccurate information. They have the ability to submit a Universal Data Form ( UDF ) to delete the inquiry but are refusing to do so, claiming technical inability.

3. Contradiction of Policy : Google 's own terms state a hard pull occurs only " If you qualify and decide to go ahead. '' I did not go ahead. I abandoned the cart.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
case_records.narrative: I am disputing multiple inaccurate and inconsistent items being reported by the credit bureaus. These items contain conflicting dates, wrong payment histories, duplicate student loan entries, mixed-file information, and unverifiable derogatory data. I have already disputed these accounts directly with the bureaus, but they did not properly investigate and appear to have used an automated system ( e-OSCAR ) without obtaining any actual documentation from the furnishers as required under FCRA 611 ( a ) and 623 ( a ). Some accounts also show indicators of identity theft and mixed-file reporting. The information reporting on my file is not accurate, not complete, and not verifiable, and must be removed immediately.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
I am filing a complaint against Equifax for reporting multiple unauthorized hard inquiries and for failing to conduct a lawful reinvestigation. Equifax is closing my disputes the same day they are filed, which makes a true reinvestigation impossible and violates several provisions of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ). 

The unauthorized inquiries are : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX XXXX did not authorize these inquiries, did not apply for credit with these lenders, and have no relationship with these entities. Equifax has refused to verify any of these inquiries but continues to report them. 



XXXX. Equifax is closing disputes the same day, proving no reinvestigation occurred Under 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ), Equifax must conduct a reasonable reinvestigation. Closing disputes on the same day shows that : No furnisher was contacted No documentation was reviewed No reinvestigation occurred This is an automated e-OSCAR denial, not an FCRA-compliant reinvestigation. 



XXXX. Equifax is not forwarding my dispute to the furnishers as required XXXX XXXX ( a ) ( XXXX ), Equifax must forward all relevant information

case_records.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
case_records.narrative:
I am writing to formally dispute the accuracy of certain late payment entries appearing on my credit report. These entries do not accurately reflect my payment history. I have previously submitted multiple requests for investigation, along with all required documentation, yet the inaccuracies have not been corrected. The continued reporting of these erroneous entries has caused me significant financial hardship and emotional distress. 

Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), I am entitled to a fair, accurate, and complete credit report. This includes the right to have any information that is inaccurate, incomplete, or unverifiable promptly corrected or removed. Despite my repeated attempts to resolve this matter, these inaccurate entries continue to appear on my report without justification or adequate explanation. 

I am therefore requesting that you conduct a thorough and immediate investigation into the disputed late payment entries. Please review the documentation I have provided, verify the accuracy of the information with the furnisher, and update or remove any entries that can not be fully verified or that inaccurately reflect my payment history.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative: I am submitting this complaint because TransUnion is reporting a XXXX XXXX hard inquiry dated XX/XX/year> without providing any written explanation or documentation showing that the inquiry was made under a valid permissible purpose as required by federal law. I have requested verification, yet the inquiry remains on my credit file without justification. Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, specifically 15 U.S.C. 1681b, access to a consumer credit report is only permitted for specific legally defined purposes. TransUnion is also required under 15 U.S.C. 1681e ( b ) to follow reasonable procedures to ensure maximum accuracy of the information they publish. Reporting an inquiry without verifying legal authorization fails that requirement. Hard inquiries impact a consumers credit profile and have real consequences. They influence approvals, interest rates, and financial standing. Allowing inquiries to remain without proof of lawful purpose is not acceptable. I am requesting enforcement assistance to require TransUnion to either produce documentation proving lawful access or delete the inquiry immediately. My credit file should not contain unverified inquiries, and I expect compliance

case_records.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
case_records.narrative:
Permissible Purpose : The law says no one can report information about me without a permissible purpose and my authorization. These accounts and inquiries have neither. There's no written agreement, no verifiable consent, nothing proving I authorized them. Continuing to report them is unlawful. On XX/XX/year>, I sent a formal dispute to TransUnion about unauthorized and inaccurate information on my credit report. I wasn't just making a casual complaint- I sent them everything they needed, including my FTC Report ( Report ID XXXX ), which is official documentation proving these accounts aren't mine. 
That was over 30 days ago. More than enough time for them to investigate and fix this. But when I checked my credit report recently, nothing has changed. The unauthorized information is still there, still damaging my credit, still affecting my life. 
This isn't just a delay or an oversight anymore. This is non-compliance with federal law. TransUnion had a legal obligation to investigate my dispute and remove information they couldn't verify. They didn't do it. 
I've done everything right. I followed their process, sent documentation, gave them the legal timeframe. They've done nothing.

My vehicle financed through Wells Fargo Auto was declared a total loss on or around [ XX/XX/year> ], and my insurance company is currently processing the claim and payoff. 

Despite this, I was charged for a payment and Wells Fargo reported the account as having a missed payment, which has negatively impacted my credit.

This missed payment is inaccurate, as the account should be handled appropriately during an active insurance claim. 

I am requesting that Wells Fargo Auto : Correct the inaccurate credit reporting Remove the missed payment from my credit report Review and properly update my account to reflect total loss status Ensure no further negative reporting occurs during this process

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 31 consumers affected by the same pattern at TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 31 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.
Customers cannot reach a human to question or reverse an automated decision — an unreasonable barrier to support and redress.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dispute resolution (e-OSCAR-style) is likely closing investigations without genuine human review, leaving errors uncorrected.
hypotheses.evidence: 31 complaints showing automated-investigation and unresolved-dispute cues; concentrated at TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC., EQUIFAX, INC., Experian Information Solutions Inc..

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

clusters.name: Mortgages — Mortgage, Rocket, Form — Trouble during payment process
sample_narratives:
Subject : Mishandling of Loan Recast Request by NewRez Details : I purchased a home XXXX months ago, and my mortgage servicing was transferred to NewRez. 

On XX/XX/year>, I deposited {$80000.00} to initiate a loan recast. 

Since then, I have received contradictory and inaccurate information from multiple NewRez representatives : On XX/XX/year>, a representative told me the recast was complete. 

The online account still reflected the old payment amount. 

The automated system says the recast is complete, but live agents say it is still pending. 

I was told I must pay a {$250.00} processing fee, but representatives gave conflicting instructions ( deduct from deposit vs. pay separately ). 

A supervisor promised a XXXX email, but I never received it. 

On XX/XX/year>, I was told a mailed acknowledgment would arrive next week. 

Impact : I have been misled repeatedly, wasting significant time and effort. 

The recast process has been delayed for over XXXX weeks despite my timely deposit. 

The companys contradictory communication has caused confusion and financial uncertainty. 

Requested Resolution : Immediate completion of the recast with correct monthly payment adjustment. 

Written confirmation of the recast terms. 

Assurance that fees and deposits are properly applied. 

Investigation into XXXX customer service practices.

Beginning around 3032 days past due, I attempted multiple times to make my XXXX payment, but every payment channel blocked me. In addition, during the last week of XXXX, I again attempted to make my XXXX payment before the 60-day mark. I was sick with a XXXX XXXX  and was not able to even pick up the phone throughout XXXX. Despite being ill, I still attempted to pay through the automated phone system, the online portal, and the guest pay function and all of them continued to fail before XX/XX/XXXX. 
This shows that I made repeated, good-faith efforts to cure the delinquency before 60 days, and that it was the servicers system failuresnot my actionsthat prevented payment. 
After failed attempts I emailed XXXX customer care team around XX/XX/XXXX XXXX am explain the issues. I did not receive a response until XX/XX/XXXX. There were contact information that was inaccurate and other reps from XXXX expressed I was told misleading information. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX  XXXX  emailed me in response to my email ( the gist ) After 32 days, the portal and guest pay would no longer work therefore I could not make my payment.I could call in to make a payment with a representative.I could mail a payment or use wire transfer etc.There was no mention of a 60-day restriction or any FHA rule prohibiting them from accepting one past-due payment. 

Therefore, I followed their written instructions exactly. I called in as as soon a a got their email. The issue is that as the system was still not letting me bypass with my loan number. I have to find a way to block my number from being recognized and then request a call back from a rep. When a rep called back after XXXX hours of waiting I explained the issue to her. 
After calling, the XXXX representative told me : She can take XXXX but then put me on hold and proceeded to say she could not accept my XXXX payment unless I paid XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX all together.She insisted she could not take a one-month payment. She attempted to push a repaym

On XX/XX/year>, I spoke with a manager named XXXX who alleged that Payment Deferral option is not available for my husbands VA loan. When I asked her to please provide any documents that show this policy that VA loans are not able to do a Payment Deferral, she refused to do so and hung up on me. 
I called back and spoke with XXXX who said an algorithm decides whether we qualify for Payment Deferral option, and refused to provide me with the information as to what documents are required and criteria considered for this option.

I escalated this issue to a second manager by the alleged name of XXXX XXXX, who stated that Payment Deferral option is available for VA loans but only for XXXX XXXX XXXX members. When I told him that my husband was XXXX and XXXX XXXX in XXXX, he then switched back to saying that Payment Deferral is not available for all VA loans. They all refused to provide me with documents that show that VA loans do not qualify for Payment Deferral option. 
I have reason to believe that we are being targeted because of my husbands military status.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 16 consumers affected by the same pattern at Rocket Mortgage, LLC and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 16 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers cannot reach a human to question or reverse an automated decision — an unreasonable barrier to support and redress.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated underwriting / affordability logic or an automated rate-switching process appears to be producing detriment without a clear, individualised rationale.
hypotheses.evidence: 16 complaints showing automated underwriting and rate-switch cues; concentrated at Rocket Mortgage, LLC, Shellpoint Partners, LLC, Data Mortgage Inc..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Rocket Mortgage, LLC for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Rocket Mortgage, LLC directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

sample_narratives: I am a lendee with HomeloanServ on a repayment plan with the lender. I attempted numerous times to make payment before deadline and was met with great barriers with their payment options just to make a payment and believe they are intentionally doing this in bad faith to roll lendees into ongoing repayment plans. I have voiced my concern with representatives prior about the issue with their payment options which have been ignored. The only option to repay is via phone with no online options. On XX/XX/XXXX I attempted to make payment with their automated system via phone and was met with an error. On XX/XX/XXXX & XX/XX/XXXX I tried to contact a representative with no answer as well as attempt to use their automated system. All 3 days did not work, when I was finally able to contact a HomeLoanServ representative on XXXX ( after waiting on hold for over 1 hour ), I was told that since I did not complete payment before deadline that my repayment plan has been rejected and that I would need to apply for a new repayment plan. When I explained that I tried to make payment numerous times before the deadline and this should be investigated or appealed, I was advised that I can only speak to a supervisor. After being on hold again, I was then told by the HomeLoanServ representative that there too many escalations from others to speak to their supervisors and I would have to wait possibly 3 days to receive a response from a supervisor. I informed the representative to note that I already attempted to pay dues prior to deadline and HomeloanServ failed to successfully give me options for paying plan successfully with errors in automated sytem as well as unable to speak with reps. I am filing a complaint with HomeloanServ due to their bad faith business practice and intentionally ignoring payment complications with their systems and then penalizing the lendees. Not sure if this is the first complaint, but I am voicing this concern with them.

clusters.name: Consumer Credit — Year, Payments, Citibank — Can't stop withdrawals from your bank account
sample_narratives: On Monday, XX/XX/year>, I connected with chat on the Dave app & asked that my ACH be stopped & that they help me make arrangements to pay back my balance. The AI chatbot connected me to a human customer service rep. I explained to them that Im disabled and that I have XXXX XXXX I have a BIG copay for meds that Im going to have to make and I need my ACH stopped & to make arrangements to pay back my balance. I was told they couldnt do that because I had a balance. I told them that this is illegal, that its law that I can ask to have it stopped & they had to work with me to pay back my balance. They kept refusing and repeating that they couldnt stop the ACH because I have a balance. While I was on the phone with them, I texted my cousin, who is an attorney. He told me that what they were doing is illegal and to tell them that my attorney would be in touch and that I was going to file a complaint with you all & my state attorney generals office. I told them this. They told me that they would get me in touch with a supervisor and that the supervisor would email me. About XXXX hours later, I got an email from a supervisor repeating THE SAME INFORMATION the chat reps had. It said to reply to the email if I had any more questions. I did, but never received a reply.

Between November 1921, 2025, I used XXXX for four purchases : Three XXXX XXXX gift cards ( {$500.00}, {$500.00}, {$300.00} ) XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX order ( {$280.00} ) As a non-California resident, XXXX own policy states that the second installment must fall between 8 and 14 days after purchase when Preferred Payment Day is active. 

However, all four of my orders were scheduled at the extreme minimum end of the rangeonly 9 to 11 days after purchase. This is significantly shorter than the usual 14-day schedule I have always received on prior Pay-in-4 orders. 

I did not knowingly activate a Sunday Preferred Payment Day, and I was never clearly warned that this setting could shorten my payment window to the minimum limit rather than the typical 14 days. Because it was not clearly disclosed, I reasonably expected my installments to follow the normal ~14-day grace period. 

After seeing the incorrect dates, I contacted XXXX before any payments were due to report the problem. I also submitted screenshots showing the shortened schedule. case_records.narrative:
I entered into a deferred interest financing agreement with PatientFi in connection with a service provided through XXXX. 

While a promotional expiration date was disclosed at the outset, it was not reasonably accessible or visible throughout the life of the loan. The deadline was primarily buried within account statements rather than clearly displayed in my account dashboard. While the promotional expiration date may have appeared on the general account homepage, it was not displayed within the specific loan detail page where a borrower would reasonably expect to find key loan terms. This inconsistency made it difficult to reliably track the deadline. They told me the date was removed after the deadline passed, so I could have kept paying the 30 % interest without any idea.

At no point did I receive any reminder, notification, or alert that the promotional expiration date was approaching. This lack of notice is particularly concerning given the severe consequence of missing the deadline.

I paid the balance in full within 11 days after the promotional expiration, demonstrating clear intent to comply with the promotional terms and always paid my bill on time. Despite this, Patien
