assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 15 consumers affected by the same pattern at CITIBANK, N.A. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated adverse actions, automated loops, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 15 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated affordability / credit-decisioning model was likely recalibrated, raising decline rates. Because such models lean on proxy variables, there is a real risk of indirect discrimination.
hypotheses.evidence: 15 complaints showing automated decline and affordability cues; concentrated at CITIBANK, N.A., Dave Operating, LLC, Block, Inc..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to CITIBANK, N.A. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask CITIBANK, N.A. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Consumer Credit — Application, Action, Denial — Getting a line of credit
sample_narratives: I am requesting CFPB investigation into Navy Federal Credit Union for unlawful and deceptive lending practices after it denied my {$3000.00} personal loan application based on vague, generic credit report information that Navy Federal did not independently verify, validate, or meaningfully review. The denial appears to have been generated through automated system decisioning rather than a lawful, individualized underwriting analysis, in violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1681 ), the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1691 ), and the Consumer Financial Protection Acts prohibition on Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ( 12 U.S.C. 5531 ). Navy Federal failed to disclose specific tradelines, verified data points, dates, or material deficiencies relied upon, while presenting the systems output as a factual basis for denial despite its inaccuracy. This conduct is unfair and deceptive because it relies on unverified data, abusive because it deprives the consumer of the ability to dispute or correct alleged information, and unlawful because it masks automated decisioning as legitimate underwriting. These actions suggest systemic compliance failures, improper reliance on third-party or algorithmic data, and disregard for statutory accuracy and disclosure obligations, warranting immediate CFPB supervisory and enforcement review.

Failure to provide full refunds including interest on optional add-on products, per CFPB enforcement action And other illegal action.

clusters.name: Consumer Credit — Check, Usaa, Stop — Getting the loan
sample_narratives:
USAA Federal Savings bank approved a personal loan and issued a USAA cashiers check via overnight mail for the loan proceeds. I deposited the cashiers check into my local bank account. After the deposit USAA placed a stop payment on the check. USAA provided no explanation for the stop payment despite my requests. I was not notified in advance and the check had already been deposited when the stop payment occurred. This action has caused financial risk and incurred stop payment fee and has left me without clarity as to whether the stop payment was an internal error or compliance action. I am requesting written confirmation of why the check for the funds was stopped, whether the loan was formally rescinded, and confirmation that I am not responsible for any returned-item fees or negative reporting to this issue. As of today I have not received any written explanation from USAA. My bank has indicated the return was issuer-initiated.

case_records.narrative: I took a home equity loan from XXXX bank and they required I pay off two other loans -- one of which was a personal loan with Barclays Bank. The titling agency mailed me a paper check to send to Barclays which I immediately sent via USPS as soon as I received the paper check. I have the USPS tracking receipt and the paper check was delivered last month. I have contacted Barclays many times to understand where the check is and why is has not been applied to my account. Meanwhile, interest is accruing and I am making additional payments. The irony is that XXXX wanted this loan to be paid off in full before taking out this new home equity loan and now I have two loans open both accruing interest. I maintain a XXXX credit score so I take this all very seriously. No one is helping me and I keep getting the run around.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 4 consumers affected by the same pattern at UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 4 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated affordability / credit-decisioning model was likely recalibrated, raising decline rates. Because such models lean on proxy variables, there is a real risk of indirect discrimination.
hypotheses.evidence: 4 complaints showing automated decline and affordability cues; concentrated at UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION, WELLS FARGO & COMPANY, BARCLAYS BANK DELAWARE.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Credit Reporting — Fraudulent, Identity, Theft — Incorrect information on your report
sample_narratives:
I am filing this complaint against XXXX XXXX  for an administrative error that resulted in a wrongful account closure and subsequent damage to my credit standing. On the XXXX, XXXX abruptly closed my XXXX  account ( Limit : {$10000.00} ), which I had just opened a week prior, citing 'suspicious activity ' related to retail purchases ( my XXXX XXXX  purchases from XXXX XXXX ). Despite my immediate dispute on the XXXX, I was told the decision was final and not to call for 30 days. 

This wrongful closure caused a nearly XXXX drop in my credit score and led to a 'Serious Delinquency ' denial from XXXX XXXX and XXXX and other creditors for which I had to use my personal credit to guarantee my business. 

The temporary 'Closed by Grantor ' status and high utilization caused measurable financial harm. I believe this was an automated risk algorithm error that XXXX failed to manually review in a timely manner and subsequently attempted to wrongfully destroy my credit. I lost nearly XXXX points because they not only inquired. 

Very unfair.

I am submitting this complaint to formally document ongoing issues involving inaccurate and fraudulent accounts reported by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, as well as procedural problems that have prevented me from obtaining a fair resolution through the CFPB complaint process. These issues include improper handling of disputes, failure to investigate identity theft claims, lack of response from one institution, and the improper designation of my follow-up complaint as a duplicate, which has prevented further review. 

Background and XXXX of the Problem I first became aware of serious issues with my credit reports after reviewing them and noticing derogatory accounts that I did not recognize and did not authorize. These included a negative auto-related account reported by XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX and a derogatory account reported by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX . I did not open these accounts, did not sign any agreements related to them, and did not receive or benefit from any products or services associated with them. Because the presence of multiple derogatory accounts that I did not recognize raised concerns of fraud or unauthorized use of my personal information, I took immediate steps to investigate an
d dispute these accounts.

Actions Taken Regarding XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX On XX/XX/year>, I filed a formal complaint with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) against XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. In that complaint, I explained that I did not own the account being reported, that I had not authorized any lease or financing arrangement, and that the account appeared to be fraudulent. 

On XX/XX/year>, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX submitted a response to my CFPB complaint. Their response stated that they believed the account was valid and that I was not a victim of identity theft. However, their response did not include original signed contracts, verified identity documentation, or any conclusive evidence showing that I personally opened or authorized the account. 

Despite

This complaint concerns the continued inaccurate reporting and handling of a fraudulent account associated with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, which I do not own, did not open, and did not authorize. I am a verified victim of identity theft, and despite filing an FTC Identity Theft Report and submitting supporting documentation, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX has refused to properly correct the inaccurate information. Instead, the company has relied on misleading, legally irrelevant assertions to avoid compliance with federal consumer protection laws. 

This complaint is being filed as a new case because a prior CFPB complaint, case number XXXX, was closed without resolving the underlying issue. The harm caused by this fraudulent account remains ongoing. 

Background of the Dispute At some point, a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX account appeared on my consumer credit reports. I immediately recognized that this account was not mine. I never applied for, opened, authorized, or benefited from any XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX product. I did not sign any agreement, provide consent, or receive any funds related to this account. 

Upon discovering the fraudulent account, I took prompt and appropriate action as required under federal law. This included : Filing an Identity Theft Report with the Federal Trade Commission ( FTC ) through IdentityTheft.gov Submitting an identity theft affidavit, sworn under penalty of law Disputing the account with consumer reporting agencies Filing a complaint with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) These actions are exactly what identity theft victims are instructed to do under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and the Fair and Accurate Credit Transactions Act ( FACTA ). 

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Improper Response Rather than complying with its legal obligations, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX responded by asserting that the FTC Identity Theft Report I submitted seems to have been modified and is not the form generated by IdentityTheft.gov. 

This statement is misleading, unsup

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
I am submitting this Identity Theft Report because credit inquiries and accounts were opened in my name without my knowledge, authorization, or consent. I am the victim of identity theft, and this statement explains how I discovered the fraud, what actions I have takenincluding filing a police reportand why the fraudulent accounts and inquiries must be removed from my credit file. Everything provided in this statement is accurate to the best of my knowledge. 

I first realized something was wrong when I reviewed my credit report and noticed several inquiries and account-related activities that were completely unfamiliar. These inquiries came from lenders and companies with which I have never applied, interacted, or shared my personal information. Before seeing these entries, I had no reason to believe my identity had been compromised. I have always taken care to protect my personal information, so seeing unauthorized inquiries immediately raised concern. After confirming that I had not applied for any of the accounts listed, it became evident that I was a victim of identity theft. 

None of the accounts or credit applications reflected on my report were initiated or approved by me.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative: I am reporting multiple inaccurate and fraudulent items appearing on my credit report. These accounts and/or inquiries were not authorized by me, and I have attached the documents that support my identity-theft claim. I previously notified all three credit bureaus and the furnishers, but they have not conducted a lawful investigation and have failed to respond within the FCRA mandated timelines. This situation has caused me significant stress, fear, and emotional frustration. It is overwhelming to see fraudulent accounts tied to my name and to feel ignored after doing everything the law requires me to do. These inaccurate items are affecting my ability to move forward financially, care for my family, and maintain stability. I have been trying to correct something I did not create, yet Im the one suffering the consequences. No one should have to fight this hard just to clear their own name.

case_records.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
case_records.narrative:
This situation has completely turned my life upside down. I discovered someone used my information to open accounts and damage my credit, and its been an emotional and financial nightmare. Every time I try to explain to the credit bureaus or the companies involved, XXXX met with silence or excuses. Im not just a number Im a real person trying to recover from something I didnt cause. Please help me get these fraudulent items removed so I can rebuild my life and protect my future. 

I'm writing because my identity has been stolen, and it has caused me more stress than I could have ever imagined. Ive worked hard to build my credit and protect my name, but now Im being held responsible for accounts and debts that arent mine. Ive done everything I can to prove my innocence, but I feel like Im being ignored by the companies reporting this false information. I just want my name cleared and my peace of mind back. No one should have to fight this hard to be seen as themselves again.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
A few weeks ago I reviewed my credit report and discovered several accounts and hard inquiries that I never authorized. There is no document proving that I ever gave permission for these entities to access my information or report anything on my file. 
When I saw this, I became concerned because it had already affected me without my knowledge. I was denied a loan I needed, and on another financing I received a higher interest rate than I should have. I was being harmed by this situation. 
I did what the law requires I filed a report with the FTC and submitted it directly to the credit bureau. Under FCRA 605B, when a consumer submits that report, the bureau has XXXX business days to block the disputed information, unless it can prove with actual documentation that the consumer authorized those accounts. In my case, that proof does not exist, because I never authorized anything. 
Despite completing the entire process, the information is still appearing on my report. The bureau has not shown me any evidence that I authorized those accounts or inquiries. 
Furthermore, FCRA 604 is clear : no one can access my credit history without a permissible purpose. These inquiries were made withou

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dispute resolution (e-OSCAR-style) is likely closing investigations without genuine human review, leaving errors uncorrected.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.

clusters.name: Mortgages — Payoff, Release, Lien — Trouble during payment process
sample_narratives:
In early XX/XX/year>, my Home Equity Line of Credit ( HELOC ) with Fifth Third Bank was frozen due to an identity theft alert. I immediately resolved the identity theft issue, removed the fraud alerts and freezes from all three credit bureaus, and my credit score has since fully recovered to above XXXX. 

Despite providing all requested documentation and repeatedly contacting Fifth Third Bank over the past 2.5 months, my HELOC remains frozen and inaccessible. I have been told that the system or computers are preventing the line from being reactivated, and that no one can provide a timeline or explanation for the continued freeze. Supervisors have also been unable to give any status updates or resolution path. 

This ongoing freeze is causing financial harm because I use my HELOC as my primary mortgage-replacement product. I am unable to make principal-lowering transfers or access my available credit, forcing me to keep funds idle in checking instead of reducing my balance. I estimate this has caused unnecessary interest expenses for several months. 

I am requesting CFPB assistance to obtain a clear explanation from Fifth Third Bank and a timely resolution of the HELOC freeze. I would like the bank to either ( 1 ) immediately reinstate normal draw and payment functionality, or ( 2 ) provide a specific reason and timeline for continued restriction.

I paid off my HELOC mortgage with Mr. Cooper. They sent me only a photocopy of the lien release. The county requires the original wetink notarized release. When I call Mr. Cooper, I can not reach a representative, and the automated system says it will take 39 months to send the original. This delay prevents me from clearing the title. In addition, I asked for a payoff quote, but they gave me a quote a day later than the servicable date so I added extra money to cover whatever interest was being added. They have not paid difference back to me.

I had already prepaid interest through XX/XX/XXXX, and as of XX/XX/XXXX, my principal balance was only one cent. Because mortgage interest accrues solely on the outstanding principal, no additional interest should have accrued after that date regardless of when the payoff was processed. However, UWMs automated system generated two different payoff statementsone in XXXX and another in XXXX significantly different refund amounts. The XX/XX/XXXX payoff statement showed that UWM owed me {$21000.00}, while the XX/XX/XXXX payoff statement showed only {$16000.00}, a difference of about {$5500.00}. The sole change was the interest to payoff date, even though my principal remained effectively zero. This discrepancy strongly suggests that UWMs system misapplied its standard payoff logic to your unique situation, calculating additional interest that could not have accrued.

The communications with UWM show that I made good-faith efforts to clarify and resolve the issue. After paying down the loan to a near-zero balance, I requested guidance on how to complete the payoff. UWMs customer service initially directed me to call, and upon doing so, I was told to generate a payoff statement. The XXXX payoff statement contained instructions only for borrowers who owe funds to UWM, such as wiring payment, but offered no direction for cases where the lender owes a refund to the borrower. Because of that lack of guidance, the payoff process was not initiated until I followed up again in XXXX, at which point UWM recalculated the payoff amount using its automated system and reduced your refund. UWM later confirmed through written messages that the payoff was processed only after your XXXX call, that the refund was {$16000.00}, and that its system calculates interest through the payoff date. However, the company never addressed the fact that no additional interest could accrue on a {$0.00} principal. Their explanation that the payoff includes the interest to the payoff date is not factually

After receiving full payoff funds, NewRez/Shellpoint issued me a refund for an overpayment, which included the same {$83.00} fee that had already been quoted and collected. Despite being paid in full, the account was not closed and the lien was not released. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, I discovered that the lien was still active and preventing my home sale from proceeding. I was then required to pay the {$83.00} fee a second time to initiate closure of the account. The account was subsequently closed effective XX/XX/XXXX, instead of XX/XX/XXXX when the payoff was actually completed.

NewRez/Shellpoint is now treating this as a new payoff and is delaying the lien release by 4060 days. However, their automated system indicates the lien release was already sent to the county on XX/XX/XXXX, with an expected release date of XX/XX/XXXX, and document availability on XX/XX/XXXX. clusters.name: Cards & Payments — Bilt, Support, Rewards — Other features, terms, or problems
sample_narratives:
Improper Account Closure Without Adequate Notice, Failure to Restore Account, and Unfair Denial of New Application by Bilt / XXXX XXXX To Whom It May Concern : I am submitting this complaint regarding the improper closure of my Bilt credit card account, issued by XXXX XXXX, and the refusal by both Bilt and XXXX XXXX to reinstate the account or allow me to reapply. Their actions were unreasonable, unfair, and have caused harm to my credit experience.

Summary of the Issue I previously held a Bilt credit card and had an excellent history of on-time payments across all my credit card accounts with multiple banks. I have never missed payments, never carried delinquent balances, and have maintained responsible credit usage.

During a residential move, I did not receive the physical mail notices from Bilt or XXXX XXXX reminding me about a balance payment. This was the only instance in which a timing issue occurred, and it was due entirely to the address change and lack of proper notice. 

Despite having no prior late payments with XXXX XXXX or Bilt, and despite my consistently strong payment record with all creditors, Bilt immediately and permanently closed my credit card account after a single incident, without offering any grace period or reasonable opportunity to correct the situation. This response was excessively harsh and disproportionate. 

Following this unexpected closure : I contacted Bilt, who stated that the closure was entirely handled by XXXX XXXX. 

I contacted XXXX XXXX, who stated the decision was made by Bilt.

Both parties refused to assist, repeatedly redirecting me back and forth without providing a solution.

Later, when I attempted to submit a new application, I was denied automatically with no meaningful review or explanation.

This pattern constitutes unfair and abusive behavior, especially given that : I had never been late on payments with them before. 

The missed notice occurred due to a change of address, and I never received any payment remi

During the Bilt transition to Bilt XXXX, I signed up for Bilt XXXX on XX/XX/year> after XXXX, CDT. I did receive a confirmation email of application approval. I immediately submitted and inquiry online and called on XX/XX/year> at XXXX XXXX  to inquire about cancelling my application for Bilt XXXX. The bank representative checked and said they sent the cancellation and confirmed I would receive an email of confirmation as a prove, and it wouldn't impact my credit history or score. I never received any email and on XXXX XXXX and XXXX, XXXX, I tried calling multiple times ( see attachment ), no one ever answering. The automatic assistant indicated estimated wait time was 10 minutes, but I have been waiting for an hour EACH time and they said, " no agent is available at this time '' and then hung up automatically. 

I received the email reply on XX/XX/year> and they did not propose any resolution, just said they would escalate the issue/involve this to others who are capable of resolving. And never receive a proper response. 

I received my Bilt XXXX XXXX XXXX around XX/XX/year> XXXX

I am filing a formal complaint against Bilt Technologies , Inc. ( Bilt Rewards ) for deceptive promotional practices, false advertising, and executive negligence regarding a financial liability tied to the Bilt Mastercard XXXX. 

I have been trying for weeks to obtain customer service to be ignored or occasionally placated with a few sentences of outdated information.

1. Executive Negligence & Financial Liability : During the transition of my Bilt XXXX from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, a {$500.00} ledger discrepancy occurred. Bilt Vice President XXXX XXXX explicitly instructed me in writing to " dispose '' of a physical {$500.00} bank-issued refund check from XXXX XXXX , claiming Bilt would reconcile it on the backend. Discarding a legal bank document leaves a {$500.00} discrepancy on a regulated ledger and exposes me to automated clawbacks. I proposed a standard accounting solution to XXXX XXXX : I cash the XXXX XXXX check to XXXX  out their ledger, and Bilt charges my new card {$500.00} to offset their digital credit. Following this proposal, XXXX XXXX completely ceased communication, willfully ignoring weeks of follow up calls and emails and leaving my account in financial limbo.

2. Deceptive Rewards Practices & Promotional Fulfillment : Bilt owns and operates a rewards program that actively fails to deliver promised value, functioning as a bait and switch.

On three separate occasions, Bilt deposited high value " Close Friends '' rewards ( including an XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and a XXXX XXXX  ) into my account as " collection credits '' that had absolutely no clickable interface, causing them to expire worthless. 

Bilt support claimed the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  was " sold out '' to justify not fulfilling my earned reward, only to push a restock notification to the app for that exact item days later, proving the inventory claim was false.

Furthermore, I successfully completed a XXXX point social media promotion for the " Close Friends '' tier by executing unboxin

case_records.narrative:
I am filing this complaint regarding several recent changes to the Bilt credit card and rewards program that were implemented without adequate notice, as well as the inability to obtain support from the company to resolve the issue. 

Historically, I used my Bilt credit card to pay my monthly rent. The rent charge would appear on my credit card statement, and I would pay the balance when my statement was due, just as with any other credit card purchase. Recently, Bilt changed its system so that rent payments are no longer processed through the credit card and are instead directly debited from a linked checking account. 

I was not notified of this change in advance. I only discovered the change on XX/XX/XXXX ( rent day ) when the payment process behaved differently than expected. This created confusion and significantly changed the financial utility of the card, as well as completely wiped out my checking account, which I was expecting to happen. 

After encountering this issue, I attempted to contact Bilt customer support. I have now been waiting over 10 days ( 8+ business days ) for any meaningful response. The only support currently available appears to be an automated AI chatbo

I have been trying to get this resolved for weeks. Nobody will answer and help you. Its very frustrating when you just need a simple task done. I just want to leave experian at this point.

This specific account represents an invaluable XXXX positive relationship with a low balance ( {$180.00} on a {$1000.00} limit ) and an exceptional payment history ( only XXXX late payments in nearly a decade ). Because XXXX XXXX XXXX submitted an unauthorized, automated challenge to a perfectly health

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 2 consumers affected by the same pattern at Experian Information Solutions Inc. and others, and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system.
The pattern repeats across 2 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Repeated automated detriment calls into question whether the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes (cross-cutting duty to avoid foreseeable harm).

hypotheses.mechanism: A recently deployed or changed automated decisioning process appears to be producing the recurring detriment seen in these complaints.
hypotheses.evidence: 2 complaints showing repeating automated-handling cues; concentrated at Experian Information Solutions Inc., Sky Blue Financial Services, Inc..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Experian Information Solutions Inc. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Experian Information Solutions Inc. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Credit Reporting — Times, Phone, Like — Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
sample_narratives:
XXXX has been reporting delinquent to my credit for the since XX/XX/XXXX. I have never received a statement or bill stating what if anything is owed/due. I have called multiple times, ( XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX ) and have sat on hold for hours while working and no one comes to the phone. I tried the automated system ( XX/XX/XXXX ) and there is no record of an account with any of my identifying information ( social, phone number, DOB ). I have received " robo '' calls/messages from the company with no direct call back information to speak with someone about this account. Nothing is connecting, no customer service assistance and I don't want to give my social security number by mail to a company that feels like a scam this company. 

During my investigation into this company and my student loans, XXXX does not show this company listed as XXXX of my loan servicers. Which raises another red flag into the operational and business practices of this company. No original loan balance, no interest, and/or payment information was provided showing that XXXX is the correct loan servicer. 

Legally, I am intitled to a formal bill with a regular billing cycle with payment information and interest rate ( if applicable ) provided and I have not received any of these things. 

This company feels like a scam with no identifying information and no statements being mailed to show any balance or payment justification. My credit has taken a huge hit without a valid reason. How can this company be allowed to report on individuals credit profile without providing valid proof of ownership and documentation with a opportunity to rectify the account or produce information.

I have received " robo '' calls/messages from the company with no direct call back information to speak with someone about this account. Nothing is connecting, no customer service assistance and I don't want to give my social security number by mail to a company that feels like a scam this company.

Legally, I am intitled to a formal bill with a regular billing cycle with payment information and interest rate ( if applicable ) provided and I have not received any of these things. 

This company feels like a scam with no identifying information and no statements being mailed to show any balance or payment justification. My credit has taken a huge hit without a valid reason. How can this company be allowed to report on individuals credit profile without providing valid proof of ownership and documentation with a opportunity to rectify the account or produce information.

My information was hacked. Someone in a different state opened a credit card with. Continental Finance using my information. I have been disputing this since it showed up on my credit report in XXXX. This is a third party that contracts with banks to approve cards. The card was approved through XXXX XXXX XXXX I sent in documents proving I don't live where the card was sent to. I had to resend it because the company failed to change their address. I never received anything saying that they received it. ( I think they sent it to the fraud address ) I finally found a phone number for Continental Finance. As proof this is not my card, the automated system did not recognize my phone number or zip code. To talk to a person I had to say it was stolen. 
This was around XXXX weeks ago and I still haven't seen confirmation in writing like I asked that this is not my account. I have since filled an FTC complaint as well as a police report. All of these were submitted by email to Continental Finance. And feels unnecessary because it was a small credit limit. 
I expected a more timely resolution. To me, this company has very shady practices and I've been questioning it's legitimate since my first letter was returned.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
case_records.narrative:
I am filing this complaint because XXXX placed an {$8.00} charge-off on my account with no explanation, and then closed my XXXX XXXX account by creditors request, which I never authorized. 

I contacted XXXX at least 3 times by email and phone beginning within the past month, including a formal dispute and notice of error. I asked for documentation showing what this {$8.00} charge was for and why my account was negatively affected. Chime has not responded and has not provided any explanation or investigation. 

Under Regulation XXXX, XXXX is required to investigate and provide written results within XXXX  business days of a reported error. They have ignored my dispute, refused to provide documentation, and continue to claim the charge is my responsibility without evidence.

case_records.narrative: Over a year and a half ago, Trans Union lowered my XXXX score from XXXX to XXXX, for no reason. Since then, my credit history has only gotten better. I didn't miss any payments or carry any debt during this period. So, every time Trans Union closed my complaint, I started another. If it seem like a duplicate, it is because Trans Union hasn't fixed the problem. They refuse to admit that they lowered my score for no reason. Then, CFPB send me a notice that this looks like a duplicate complaint. So, what am I suppose to do : Stop complaining about it? This is complete and utter nonsense. Trans Union needs to stop acting like a bunch of XXXX, and fix the problem they created.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 13 consumers affected by the same pattern at Experian Information Solutions Inc. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 13 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dispute resolution (e-OSCAR-style) is likely closing investigations without genuine human review, leaving errors uncorrected.
hypotheses.evidence: 13 complaints showing automated-investigation and unresolved-dispute cues; concentrated at Experian Information Solutions Inc., TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC., EQUIFAX, INC..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Experian Information Solutions Inc. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Experian Information Solutions Inc. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Credit Reporting — Experian, Phone, Tried — Unable to get your credit report or credit score
sample_narratives: Upon applying for a home equity line of credit from a XXXX XXXX they informed me that they were unable to get my credit information due to the credit report freeze on Experian. I went to the website and entered my credentials. I was informed that they weren't correct. Tried to reset information and was told that I would receive a reset link via email but it never came. Tried repeatedly never came. Tried several different emails incase I got the email wrong somehow, still no email. Tried contacting the company itself no way to speak to actual human. This went on for weeks. Finally got some help on the internet by searching for problems with Experian. And someone on XXXX said that if we entered in all XXXX  for our address and other information the automated system would send us to an agent. Well that worked. The person told us to request a new pin since we couldn't remember ours but the only way to do that was by regular mail. We had to send a copy of my id, and a utility bill in my name to verify my identity and then they will send me a new pin. I sent in the information and it has been over XXXX weeks and still haven't heard anything back. They first said it would be XXXX business days from the time they received my identity information. This has been over a month now of trying to unfreeze my credit report.

Experian is reporting inaccurate and unverifiable information on my credit report, including a bankruptcy public record and multiple old/paid accounts that Experian claims they verified, although no verification took place with the original source.

I disputed these items through Experians system. Instead of conducting a real investigation, Experian relied on an automated system to verify the items without contacting the original source of the information. Bankruptcy courts do NOT report information directly to Experian, yet Experian claims a third party certified the information. The furnisher of the information was not identified.

Under FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 7 ), I requested the Method of Verification. Experian refused to provide : The name of the furnisher The address and phone The date the item was verified The procedure used to verify the disputed information Experian simply responded that the item remains, which is not a valid response and does not satisfy federal law. 

Experian is also reporting old, outdated, or previously paid accounts inaccurately. These accounts should have been updated or removed but are still reporting incorrect information, and after dispute, Experian again refused to provide the method of verification.

Experian is in violation of : FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 1 ) Failure to conduct a reasonable investigation FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 5 ) ( A ) Failure to delete or correct unverifiable information FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 7 ) Failure to provide the Method of Verification FCRA 607 ( b ) Failure to maintain maximum possible accuracy I am unable to obtain housing, fair credit, and financial stability due to Experians negligent handling of my disputes.

case_records.issue: Unable to get your credit report or credit score
case_records.narrative: I requested my free annual credit reports from the Annual Credit Report website. Experian asked for an authorization code they texted to me, but there was no input box to enter the code. I could not get my Experian report. I went to the Experian website and had numerous issues trying to get my free report without success and could not get any customer service to respond. Next, I tried calling their customer service number, and their AI bot gave me the runaround with looping requests for information and trying to sell me my credit score. It never gave me access to my online credit report. It acted like it was clueless. I see a problem on the TransUnion report that might be identity theft, so I really feel desperate to see my Experian report today. As for TransUnion, I tried to get help about the possible identity theft issue on their website customer service, but there was no way to do it. So I tried calling them and could not get through to a real person either, and the TransUnion AI bot also gave me the runaround with requesting information to be entered over and over again but providing no help or answers. I can not get ahold of anyone, and I've spent hours on this. I am so frust

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
case_records.narrative:
I am filing this complaint because Experian violated multiple sections of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) during my dispute process. Experian refused to provide required information, misrepresented my rights, and admitted to using an automated system instead of performing a lawful investigation. 

Here is what happened : 1. Experian refused to provide the Method of Verification ( MOV ) After Experian marked an account as verified, I requested the Method of Verification under FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 6 ). 
The Experian representative told me : We dont do MOV. 

This is false and violates FCRA, which requires Experian to provide : The name of the person they contacted at the furnisher Their job/title The procedure used to verify the information The business name, address, and phone number Experian refused all of this. 

2. Experian admitted they did NOT conduct a reasonable investigation The representative stated : We just go off what the furnisher has. 

This means Experian did not : independently investigate review documentation verify evidence speak to anyone confirm accuracy They simply accepted whatever the furnisher provided. 
Courts have ruled repeatedly that this violates FCRA

case_records.issue: Problem with fraud alerts or security freezes
case_records.narrative:
I am filing this complaint because I am in the middle of an identity theft and fraud crisis, and Experian has made it nearly impossible for me to get the help I urgently need. I have spent days trying to place an extended fraud alert and freeze on my credit report, but I can not reach a single human being at Experian. Their phone system traps you inside the same repeated automated messages no matter what option you select. It is the exact same loop over and over again with no way out. Their AI refuses to connect me to a representative, and it ignores everything I say. 

My Experian account is currently locked because of the suspected identity theft activity, but the phone number listed on my credit file is old. Because of that, I can not verify my identity through their automated system. I can not update my information. I can not access my online account. I can not finish the steps required for the identity theft process. I can not speak to a human. I can not move forward. Experian has essentially trapped me in a situation where I am fully blocked from protecting myself. 

This is not a small inconvenience. This is a serious crisis. I am dealing with IRS notices, VA notices, and ot

case_records.issue: Credit monitoring or identity theft protection services
case_records.narrative:
Experian provides no means to delete one 's account or even close the account without deleting any information. The only reply I get from their chatbot is " Experian does not provide a direct option to close your online account entirely. However, you can stop receiving emails and manage your membership preferences. If you have a paid membership, you can cancel or downgrade to a free plan. For free accounts, you can manage your email preferences to stop communications. 

If youre looking for a specific way to delete your account entirely, I dont have that information available. You may need to contact Experians automated support line at XXXX for further assistance. Please note that this line is fully automated. Let me know if you need help with anything else! '' There is no means to completely halt communications with this entity.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.issue: Credit monitoring or identity theft protection services
case_records.narrative:
XXXX XX/XX/year> ( XX/XX/XXXX and the days after ), I was attempting to place a Security Freeze on my credit to prevent identity theft. I was supposed to be able to do so by creating an account with Experian. However, their system entirely prevented me from creating an account, without telling a reason or suggesting any possible solution. I tried to reach out to them by calling, but their automated-response system constantly hung up on my call and completely left me no way to talk to a representative. 

When I switched from phone to computer to sign up, I found out that I could mail or submit documents online to request the Security Freeze on my account. I personally couldn't afford mailing, so I submitted my documents online, including the request, my government-issued ID, and a bank statement verifying my address. I even explained my issue in the request and asked for their assistance. Nevertheless, there was completely no response from Experian. I waited a month, and there was no email nor mail, even though I included my email and my mailing address on the request. 

I thought my credit was not frozen due to their unresponsiveness, so I recently tried to apply for an auto loan.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
Experian is reporting inaccurate and unverifiable information on my credit report, including a bankruptcy public record and multiple old/paid accounts that Experian claims they verified, although no verification took place with the original source.

I disputed these items through Experians system. Instead of conducting a real investigation, Experian relied on an automated system to verify the items without contacting the original source of the information. Bankruptcy courts do NOT report information directly to Experian, yet Experian claims a third party certified the information. The furnisher of the information was not identified.

Under FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 7 ), I requested the Method of Verification. Experian refused to provide : The name of the furnisher The address and phone The date the item was verified The procedure used to verify the disputed information Experian simply responded that the item remains, which is not a valid response and does not satisfy federal law.

case_records.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
case_records.narrative:
On or about XX/XX/year>, I disputed the accuracy of a XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX account with Experian. Experian completed the reinvestigation and reported the account as verified. 

After receiving the dispute results, I attempted to request the Method of Verification pursuant to FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 7 ) to understand how the account was verified, including who was contacted, the method used, and what documentation was reviewed.

Experian does not provide a clear or accessible way to request a Method of Verification for a closed dispute. When I attempted to obtain guidance through Experians website chatbot, I was informed that there is no specific information available on how to request a Method of Verification for a closed dispute.

I am requesting that Experian provide the required Method of Verification or correct or delete the account if it can not be verified in compliance with the FCRA.

case_records.issue: Problem with fraud alerts or security freezes
case_records.narrative:
This is in response to the Credit Reporting business Experian and my interactions with them online on XX/XX/year> and online/calling on XX/XX/year>. 

I was recommended by other parties to set up a freeze on my credit reporting after another party tried to use my identify taking out a loan.

I attempted to sign up for an account online but their system either reports technical difficulties off and on for the past two days or informs me it can not confirm my identity and that I have to call or write to complete the process.

When I call, the automated system indicates it too has a problem but will not forward me on to a representative. Their writing method informed me I needed to send copies of identification documentation, which is not recommended from what I've learned.

Thus their refusal to allow incoming calls to access live operators leaves people that need to use their services in an impossible situation and more vulnerable such as is my case. 

Thank you.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
Your choices dont match my complaint. This is about the credit reporting company, not about misinformation received by it. 

Yesterday and today XX/XX/year> I tried to delete/close my account with Experian. After signing in and following all directions on their website including their Support Help, XXXX chat bot, and phone number given by their chat bot I am unable to locate how to close my account. The chat bot explained they didnt have the information to do that, and I had to call their automated phone system as no email or phone number were available to converse with a real person. The automated phone system said they didnt have the information and I had to do it online at XXXX, which is where I started. The chat bot didnt have an answer about how to escalate this request and offered only escalation information to complain about other companies misinformation about me.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 22 consumers affected by the same pattern at Experian Information Solutions Inc. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated loops, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 22 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dispute resolution (e-OSCAR-style) is likely closing investigations without genuine human review, leaving errors uncorrected.
hypotheses.evidence: 22 complaints showing automated-investigation and unresolved-dispute cues; concentrated at Experian Information Solutions Inc., TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC., EQUIFAX, INC..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Experian Information Solutions Inc. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Experian Information Solutions Inc. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

sample_narratives: For account number XXXX : On XX/XX/XXXX I set up two payments to come out of my checking account. One payment was for {$1000.00} and another one for {$300.00}. Then I went to make my regular monthly payment and I saw the updated past due balance. I thought both payments went through accordingly, so I did not think about further information. I received a blank notice in the mail from Mr. Cooper next confused as to why it was blank. I then called, begging for assistance as this should have never happened. I was told to email XXXX and explain my situation. for a curtesy one time waver of late fee as this would destroy my credit. I received an inquiry that this will take 30 days for review. I emailed 5 more times after this begging for help. Explaining that I am recovering from going XXXX from my auto immune XXXX and this year has taken a toll on me and I am working on getting a new mortgage. I was told a representative called us on XX/XX/XXXX but did not leave a voicemail. Then, she told me on XX/XX/XXXX the automated system called but no voicemail. I asked why I did not receive any calls within XXXX weeks and she stated that it was still processing but then confirmed that it took XXXX days to process. I became upset because all I am trying to do is better myself and I am asking for just a one-time waive of the late reporting as I have been making on time payments for almost 5 years and never late. I explained that I have gone XXXX and slowly getting my eyesight back with my team of drs. and an extensive medical condition. The woman was so rude to me. It was a team lead, stated that a manager will do the same thing as she does so its pointless to go higher than her. Then she threatened me with foreclosure. At this point I am crying my eyes out because I am just asking for a waiver. Not that I am not making my payments. I felt belittled and dehumanized within a 20-minute phone call. I'm already humiliated and I felt so mistreated. I'm just trying to better my life.. The

I attempted to login online to sign up for biweekly payments and once again was met with the same ownership validation message on XX/XX/year>. assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 11 consumers affected by the same pattern at Rocket Mortgage, LLC and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system, chatbot.
The pattern repeats across 11 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Repeated automated detriment calls into question whether the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes (cross-cutting duty to avoid foreseeable harm).

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated underwriting / affordability logic or an automated rate-switching process appears to be producing detriment without a clear, individualised rationale.
hypotheses.evidence: 11 complaints showing automated underwriting and rate-switch cues; concentrated at Rocket Mortgage, LLC, Mr. Cooper Group Inc., TRUIST FINANCIAL CORPORATION.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Rocket Mortgage, LLC for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Rocket Mortgage, LLC directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Debt Collection — Reporting, Verification, Debt — Attempts to collect debt not owed
sample_narratives:
I am filing this complaint regarding a debt being reported under the name RNT Debt with reference XXXX, which I do not recognize, do not owe, and have never authorized or entered into any agreement related to. This account has appeared on my credit report without my knowledge, consent, or lawful validation, and despite repeated efforts to obtain documentation, verification, and clarification from the involved parties, I have not received any legally required information proving the validity, ownership, or accuracy of this alleged debt.

This complaint outlines in detail what occurred, the steps I have taken, the lack of response or inadequate response from the company, and why this tradeline violates federal consumer protection laws including the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), and other regulatory standards intended to ensure accuracy, fairness, and transparency in consumer credit reporting and debt collection.

SECTION 1 INITIAL DISCOVERY OF THE ACCOUNT I discovered the RNT Debt account while reviewing my credit report. The account appeared to show a balance and payment history that I did not recognize. I do not have any recollection of entering into an agreement with this company, nor have I ever conducted business with any creditor or service provider that matches the name " RNT Debt '' or the reference number XXXX. 

Because the account was unfamiliar and unexpected, I immediately suspected it may have been : Reported in error Assigned to the wrong consumer Created due to identity theft Incorrectly transferred Attached to someone elses data Or otherwise inaccurately associated with me The presence of this account has already had negative consequences, as inaccurate derogatory information reduces creditworthiness and affects the ability to obtain credit, housing, insurance, employment opportunities, and other essential consumer functions.

SECTION 2 LACK OF VALIDATION AND FAILURE TO PROVIDE REQUIRED DOCUMENTA

I am creating a new CFPB case because my previous complaint, Case Number XXXX, was closed without resolving the central issue : Fair Collections & Outsourcing ( XXXX ) is reporting a collection account that I do not own, have never authorized, and have never received any legally required validation for. Because this account does not belong to me and because XXXX failed to validate the debt as required under federal law, I filed an FTC Identity Theft report to formally document that this account is unauthorized and fraudulent. Despite all of these steps, the account continues to be reported, which is why I must open a new case and request full CFPB review. 

The core issue is that XXXX has never provided any documentation proving that the alleged debt belongs to me. They have not provided a contract, a lease, a service agreement, a bill, a statement, or any other form of original documentation showing that I am the responsible party. Their previous response to my first CFPB complaint did not include validation. Instead, they simply stated that they will send verification, which is not compliance with FDCPA 809 ( b ). Debt collectors are required to validate BEFORE continuing to report or collect, not after they have already furnished derogatory information. Their response confirms that they reported the debt without validation, which is a violation of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act and the Fair Credit Reporting Act. 

In my original complaint, I made it very clear that I had no knowledge of this account prior to seeing it on my credit report. I never received a letter from XXXX, never received a notice of debt, never received a validation letter within XXXX days of any supposed initial communication, and never received any documents of any kind. The FDCPA is explicit in requiring debt collectors to provide written notice of the debt, including the amount owed and the name of the original creditor. XXXX never provided this notice. Instead, they furnished negat

Beginning in or around XX/XX/year>, a disputed XXXX XXXX  charge was entered into the tenant ledger through a property management software platform ( XXXX ) used by the property XXXX. Although my account simultaneously showed a credit balance and nothing for you to pay, automated notices and ledger entries continued to reflect past-due amounts. 
The disputed utility charge was treated as rent arrears and used as the basis for payment demands and eviction-related representations, despite my timely dispute and requests for clarification. I repeatedly requested a full accounting explaining how the disputed utility charge was classified, why the ledger showed both a credit and a past-due balance, and the basis for any past-due or enforcement-related notices generated by the system. 
The responses provided did not meaningfully address these discrepancies or correct the misclassification before enforcement actions proceeded. As a result, automated system-generated records were relied upon as authoritative despite notice of dispute, leading to inaccurate representations of debt allegedly owed.

case_records.issue: Attempts to collect debt not owed
case_records.narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding a XXXX XXXX account that appeared on my consumer credit report today and does not belong to me. 

I did not open, authorize, or benefit from this account. 

My residence is XXXX XXXX, and I have never had gas service of any kind. XXXX XXXX is a natural gas provider. A gas utility account can not exist for my residence. The appearance of this account therefore does not correspond to my home, utility history, or service capability. 

This account just appeared on my credit report, and I am disputing it immediately upon discovery. I am requesting that XXXX XXXX provide proof showing that I applied for, authorized, or received gas service. At this time, no such proof has been provided, including no service agreement, no application, no meter information, and no service address tying this account to me. 

Method of Verification Request If XXXX XXXX or any credit reporting agency asserts that this account has been verified, I am requesting disclosure of the Method of Verification, including : The name, address, and telephone number of the party that verified the information The date the verification was performed Whether verification was conducted

case_records.issue: Attempts to collect debt not owed
case_records.narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, I am requesting clarification for the record. 

Please identify the lawful basis, evidentiary authority, and method of verification by which you assert that I stand in the position of a borrower or debtor in relation to the account you are reporting. 

Specifically : 1. What original, wet-ink contract, bearing my knowing and voluntary signature, establishes me as a borrower rather than a creditor or lender in this transaction?

2. What competent witness with first-hand knowledge verified this account, and by what exact method ( manual review versus automated system )?

3. What proof do you possess that this obligation exists within my lawful capacity, rather than as a presumption outside covenant and consent? 

For clarity, my standing is that of a lender and not a borrower. Any claim to the contrary requires strict proof. Absent such proof, continued reporting or collection constitutes an unverified and inaccurate assertion. 

Please respond with documentary evidence, not conclusions or summaries.

I disputed this account with Experian , and it was marked as verified. However, Experian has failed to provide any description of the method of verification used, as required under FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 7 ). assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 24 consumers affected by the same pattern at Portfolio Recovery Associates, LLC and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated system.
The pattern repeats across 24 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Repeated automated detriment calls into question whether the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes (cross-cutting duty to avoid foreseeable harm).

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dialer / letter systems may be pursuing debts not owed, with no easy route to a human to correct the record.
hypotheses.evidence: 24 complaints showing debt-not-owed and automated-contact cues; concentrated at Portfolio Recovery Associates, LLC, CL Holdings LLC, FirstKey Holdings, LLC.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Portfolio Recovery Associates, LLC for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Portfolio Recovery Associates, LLC directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Debt Collection — Email, Debt, Payments — Attempts to collect debt not owed
sample_narratives: I was admitted to the hospital in XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX The Emergency Department Physician who admitted was a contracted Emergency Medicine Physician who is an employee of XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  group who staffs the Emergency Department at the time of admission. A collection was placed on all XXXX credit reports as a collection in the amount of {$1400.00}. However, this amount was paid in full along with the hospital bill by XXXX in 2024. I know this because I have documentation from XXXX which is the Summary of Benefits for the bill and amount reimbursed to XXXX for said date of service and admission. I should never have been given this bill. Someone made an error but I was penalized with having it on all 3 of my credit reports. After presenting the necessary documentation to XXXX Billing and Americollect I was told it was canceled on all 3 credit reports. I have reached out on numerous occasions to dispute this information with all 3 credit reporting companies. I have spoken extensively with XXXX on this matter. All have said no and I repeat no input or algorithm with any documentation was provided or received in their system. Therefore, this collection from Americollect will stay until they are notified by the debt collector company to remove or delete the collection. The TransUnion file XXXX XXXXXXXX and the XXXX billing account XXXX XXXXXXXX. Please ask Americollect to do their due diligence in going in and deleting this collection from my credit reports. This collection has dropped my scores XXXX points across the board on all 3 reports.

I am attempting to obtain written payoff statements for judgment accounts being handled by XXXX XXXX. 

XXXX  XXXX  automated system instructs consumers to request payoff statements via email. When I follow that instruction and submit the request by email, I receive a response stating that I must call instead. 

When I call XXXX XXXX, I am repeatedly told to allow XXXX business days for the payoff statements to be issued. I have received this same response for over a week, despite multiple follow-ups, and no payoff statements have been provided. 

Most recently, I was advised that XXXX XXXX does not provide payoff letters as part of its business practice and that I should calculate my own interest based on the judgment. 

This refusal and ongoing delay prevents me from resolving or satisfying the judgments and interferes with my ability to manage and resolve the debt. I am requesting that XXXX XXXX immediately provide written payoff statements that clearly state the total amount due, including any interest and fees, and explain why these payoff statements have not been provided despite repeated requests.

I get loads of emails from Aidvantage about a student loan debt they say they need me to pay to them. I don't even know when Aidvantage got my information. I want to know if they are legitimate, so l 've been trying to get their email in order to contact them about emailing me the information I need. 

I have looked all over the small part of their website that I can even access and there is no email info, including under the " Email '' section of their " contact us '' page. Everything you click to get to an email to contact them funnels you into a page that makes you create an account. I don't want to make an account with a company I don't know using my personal information. I just want to email them like they email me. It feels very intrusive how they have my information somehow, but I have to give them more information to maybe be allowed to reply or ask a question via email. 

I could only access an unhelpful chatbot that did not give me an email when I asked it, only another link to log in. I asked the chatbot if I could get a Debt Validation Notice, and it gave me unrelated information, gave me a link to a student loan forgiveness site that I did not ask for, and in general the chatbot did not answer any of my questions or provide any real help. I have no way to contact this company ( Aidvantage ) who claims I have to pay them money for a debt.

I tried to call Synchrony Bank but they will not talk to you on the phone, its an automated system, until you enter your social security number and Im not going to do that because this not my debt. 

After looking, the debt is on XXXX of my credit reports? An inquiry was made, it was not me! 

There is no account number on the email.

On XX/XX/year>, I received an email from XXXX XXXX attempting to collect a debt allegedly owed to Jefferson Capital Systems , LLC ( original creditor : XXXX XXXX ) in the amount of {$380.00}. 
The email violates the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act in the following ways : 1. MISSING VALIDATION NOTICE ( 15 U.S.C. 1692g ( a ) ) : The communication fails to include any of the required validation notice language. It does not state that the debt will be assumed valid unless disputed within 30 days. It does not state that if I dispute the debt in writing within 30 days, XXXX will obtain and mail verification. It does not state that I may request the original creditors name and address in writing within 30 days. Instead, the email contains only hyperlinks labeled click here and Dispute this debt without the actual required disclosures. 
2. MISSING VALIDATION PERIOD END DATE ( 12 C.F.R. 1006.34 ( c ) ( 3 ) ( iii ) ) : Regulation F requires debt collectors to disclose the date they will consider the end of the validation period. This communication does not include that date. 
3. FICTITIOUS SENDER NAME : The email is signed by XXXX XXXX. According to XXXX own website ( XXXX  ), this is not

They are not the only company to do this, however, they are the only company that calls three times a day. upon contacting them, I was never able to speak to a human being, instead shuffled from one automated system to another. Our economy and the inflation rate are bad enough without companies contributing to the erosion of my credit score. Any helped the bureau can provide would be greatly appreciated.
