assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 14 consumers affected by the same pattern at PENNYMAC LOAN SERVICES, LLC. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, artificial intelligence, automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated loops, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 14 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dialer / letter systems may be pursuing debts not owed, with no easy route to a human to correct the record.
hypotheses.evidence: 14 complaints showing debt-not-owed and automated-contact cues; concentrated at PENNYMAC LOAN SERVICES, LLC., CITIBANK, N.A., AmeriCollect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to PENNYMAC LOAN SERVICES, LLC. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask PENNYMAC LOAN SERVICES, LLC. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

clusters.name: Consumer Credit — Affirm, Payments, Canceled — Getting the loan
sample_narratives: PayPal stated that I could pay in 4 payments for two transactions i and I was charged in full for the items and not in 4 payments. I am pre-approved for these payments. When the company was contacted they said it must have been a cliche and they couldn't make a corrections to the computer error.

I booked a flight through XXXX and canceled it on XX/XX/XXXX, which was within XXXX XXXX cancellation policy, so I was entitled to a full refund. XXXX also emailed me confirming that my flight was canceled. 

Affirm shows XXXX refunds processed on XX/XX/XXXX : {$470.00} {$470.00} {$110.00} ( protection plan ) On XX/XX/XXXX, Affirm emailed me saying that a refund check for {$940.00} is in the mail. I never received any check. 

After receiving that email, Affirm automatically charged my card {$350.00} for the loan, which pushed my account negative. When I contacted them about it, they told me there was nothing they could do. 

Since then, I have contacted Affirm more than XXXX times by phone. Every time, I was told to wait for the check and that it would take up to XXXX  days, but over XXXX  days have passed and nothing has arrived. When I called again, I was told the issue would be escalated, but no one followed up. I spoke with XXXX supervisors XXXX and XXXX and both told me there was nothing they could do except escalate the case, but I still have not received any update or resolution. 

Meanwhile, my Affirm loan ( # XXXX ) still shows a balance, and Affirm has attempted to charge me multiple payments for an order that was fully refunded. It is now showing a late payment, and I am being threatened with negative credit reporting for a charge that should not exist. 

I am requesting : Verification of all refunds Affirm received from XXXX XXXX Immediate correction of my loan balance to {$0.00} Removal of any negative charges or late fees Closure of the loan Written confirmation that no negative credit reporting will occur regarding this dispute

On XX/XX/year>, I made a purchase through Affirm with XXXX XXXX XXXX for approximately {$270.00}. A duplicate order was created in error, and XXXX XXXX XXXX canceled XXXX of the purchases. The canceled order was refunded in full. 

On XX/XX/year>, Affirm applied XXXX adjustments of {$130.00} and {$130.00}, which together total the full {$270.00} refund amount. However, instead of applying the full refund to the canceled loan and closing it, Affirm split the refund across XXXX separate loans. As a result, I now have XXXX active loans and am being required to make XXXX payments within days of each other for a purchase that was canceled. 

I contacted Affirm multiple times via chat on XX/XX/year>, and was transferred between several agents and departments over more than an hour. I provided confirmation that the merchant canceled the duplicate order and that the refund emails state the refund was processed. Despite this, Affirm representatives stated the system automatically applied the refund to multiple loans and that they are unable to move or correct the funds once applied. 

A supervisor confirmed that the refund was split due to an automated system process and stated that because the adjustments were already applied, Affirm can not correct the loan balances. I was told a push notification was allegedly sent on XX/XX/year>, allowing me to manually match the refund, but I never received this notification and was not given the opportunity to apply the refund myself. I do receive Affirm emails and SMS notifications, and no notice explaining this process was provided to me. 

As a result of XXXX system error, I am being billed for XXXX loans instead of having the canceled loan fully voided. I did not authorize XXXX loans, did not agree to split refund adjustments, and can not afford duplicate payments for a canceled transaction. I am requesting correction of my account so the canceled loan is closed and the balance is adjusted to reflect that I owe {$0.00} on the cance

case_records.product: Payday loan, title loan, personal loan, or advance loan
case_records.issue: Loan payment wasn't credited to your account
case_records.narrative:
I pay my car loan over the phone on the banks automated system with my banks routing and account number ( I can not pay with a debit card ) the system tells me to wait 2-3 business days for the money to be debited. By the time I check on it the payment is late. I call the bank and now Im charged late fees!! This is unacceptable!! It becomes my word against theirs.

I believe their automated system is faulty and this is a way for them to collect unnecessary late fees when the issue becomes the lender vs the lender scenario.

According to XXXX XXXX  I can pay my loan in person with my debit card, yet Ive been to the branch and told its not true and have been forced to return with cash.

case_records.product: Payday loan, title loan, personal loan, or advance loan

Instead, the transactions were processed as pay in full, triggering an immediate attempted bank withdrawal, NSF fees, account suspension, and locked access to my Affirm card all right at check-in time, causing significant inconvenience, stress, and financial harm. After being transferred multiple times across agents ( including back to the same unhelpful representative, XXXX XXXX, proving the broken escalation loop ), support refused any manual intervention, repeatedly blaming the system and offering only empty apologies without resolution. This is not isolated ; numerous similar complaints on XXXX ( XXXX ) and XXXX describe Affirms notorious issues with hotel bookings ( especially XXXX ), where plans vanish, charges bypass installments, virtual cards expire before final charges, and disputes are ignored turning buy now, pay later into a deceptive trap. I demand Affirm immediately : ( 1 ) reverse the err

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 5 consumers affected by the same pattern at Affirm Holdings, Inc and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system, computer error.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 5 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers cannot reach a human to question or reverse an automated decision — an unreasonable barrier to support and redress.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated affordability / credit-decisioning model was likely recalibrated, raising decline rates. Because such models lean on proxy variables, there is a real risk of indirect discrimination.
hypotheses.evidence: 5 complaints showing automated decline and affordability cues; concentrated at Affirm Holdings, Inc, Paypal Holdings, Inc, FIRSTBANK PUERTO RICO.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Affirm Holdings, Inc for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Affirm Holdings, Inc directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Auto Lending — Vehicle, Repossession, Documentation
sample_narratives:
On XX/XX/scrub> 2025, my vehicle was repossessed by Santander Consumer USA at approximately XXXXXX/XX/XXXX, as confirmed by timestamped XX/XX/XXXX camera footage. I received no written notice of default, no right-to-cure notice, no notice of intent to repossess, and no opportunity to resolve any alleged issue before the repossession took place. 


AXX/XX/XXXXmore than XXXXXX/XX/XXXX AFTER my vehicle had already been takenSantander sent an email stating that my vehicle may be picked up soon. This proves that the required notices were not sent prior to the repossession and that I was not given any opportunity to cure or understand the alleged problem. 


On XX/XX/year>2025, I received only a generic your account is past due email with no cure amount, no cure deadline, no mention of default, and no warning that repossession was being pursued. This does not meet legal notice requirements. 


I was never informed that any payment had been returned or failed. I received no mailed notice, no email notice, and no communication giving me a chance to fix the issue. 


When I called Santander, their representative falsely claimed that my phone number was not on file, even though their automated system immediately recognized my number, identified my account, and authenticated me. This raises concerns about the accuracy of their internal records, and whether any notices were ever properly generated, addressed, or delivered. 


After the repossession, I attempted to contact Santander repeatedly. I faced multiple hold times exceeding XXXXXX/XX/XXXX, repeated transfers, and no ability to reach anyone who could explain why my vehicle was taken or provide any documentation. This prevented me from resolving the issue or retrieving necessary information. 


This is theXX/XX/XXXX time this has happened. A similar repossession occurred previously, also without proper notice or opportunity to cure, suggesting a repeated pattern of improper servicing and failure to maintain accurate accoun

However, every attempt to schedule an appointment was denied through the automated system. There was no phone number to reach a live person at XXXX XXXX XXXX. I called XXXX XXXX XXXX for assistance and was repeatedly told to wait and keep trying. 

I was given conflicting explanations by different representatives, including : XXXX had to clear XXXX needed management review XXXX needed multiple approvals XXXX delays Despite paying my vehicle fees in full, I was unable to retrieve my vehicle. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I was informed by XXXX XXXX that my vehicle had been transported to XXXX XXXX auction facility in Mt. XXXX, Tennessee, approximately XXXX hours away from my residence. 

This transfer occurred AFTER I paid my vehicle in full and AFTER XXXX XXXX sent the release authorization. 

I was never notified that my vehicle would be moved. 

This has caused significant hardship, stress, inconvenience, and financial burden. 

I paid my vehicle off in good faith to redeem it, yet I was denied access to my property and my vehicle was moved farther away, making retrieval significantly more difficult. 

I believe XXXX XXXX and their agent, XXXX XXXX XXXX, failed to properly handle my redemption and failed to make m

As part of the transaction, I made a down payment of approximately {$3800.00} that was processed electronically through XXXX. Carvana later claimed that this payment was reversed through a chargeback. However, my bank has confirmed that no refund or chargeback was ever returned to my account. I have repeatedly asked Carvana and XXXX for documentation proving that the funds were actually reversed, but they have refused to provide payment processor records showing that the money was returned to me. 

Approximately XXXX months after the purchase, on or about XX/XX/year>, the vehicle experienced a major transmission failure and would no longer shift into gear. When the vehicle was inspected, the transmission fluid was described as burnt, and there were also electrical defects affecting the vehicles operation. These serious issues occurred shortly after purchase even though the vehicle had been advertised as inspected and certified. 

I reported the problem to Carvana and the vehicle was taken to a repair facility. The vehicle remained in the repair shop for more than 90 days, leaving me without transportation. I had originally sold my previous vehicle in order to obtain the down payment to purchase this car, so I had no reliable way to get to work while the vehicle was inoperable. 

Carvana told me they would allow me to swap the vehicle, but only if I paid another down payment of approximately {$3800.00}. Carvana claims they do not have my original down payment because of the alleged chargeback, but they have not produced documentation showing the payment was actually reversed. Because of this, Carvana is refusing to swap a vehicle that they sold as certified and inspected unless I pay the down payment a

At the end of the lease, I received a turn-in settlement invoice that included a charge of {$1000.00} related to alleged excessive tire wear. I reviewed the inspection report and immediately addressed the issue before the vehicle return process was finalized. At that time, I was informed that the XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX tires required replacement. In order to ensure full compliance with safety requirements, mechanical integrity, and lease obligations, I replaced all XXXX tires and retained documentation confirming the replacement. 

The replacement tires met the required specifications and were installed in matched pairs with equivalent wear, appropriate speed ratings, and performance characteristics suitable for the vehicle. On XX/XX/XXXX, I paid the undisputed portion of the lease-end invoice

I am filing a complaint regarding the repossession of a vehicle belonging to my child and the conduct of both the lender and the repossession company involved. 
In the days leading up to the repossession, my child reported being followed and monitored. Based on these circumstances, there is concern that the vehicle may have been tracked or surveilled prior to repossession. I am requesting full disclosure of any such methods used. 
The vehicle was repossessed without any communication regarding the handling or retrieval of personal belongings. I was forced to initiate contact and was provided a phone number ( XXXX ) and reference number, but could not reach a live representative. The automated system directed me to an online portal that did not recognize the VIN provided, effectively preventing access to information about the vehicle. 
This lack of access to personal property and failure to provide clear retrieval instructions is unacceptable and may violate consumer protection standards. 
I am requesting : A full written report of the repossession process Documentation of any tracking or surveillance methods used Chain of custody documentation for the vehicle A complete inventory o

clusters.name: Mortgages — Active, Cooper, Payments — Trouble during payment process
sample_narratives:
Loan payments to Mr. Cooper Mortgage. I was charged twice on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX despite setting up automatic payments. My attempts to contact the company were initially unsuccessful due to their automated system, which did not allow me to speak to a representative. After considerable effort, I found that saying the word 'letter ' could connect me to a real person. 
Eventually, I managed to speak to a representative who suggested a three-way call with my bank to resolve the issue and return my money. However, during the call, the representative disconnected, forcing me to repeat the process. After more attempts, the website went down, adding to my frustration. 
When I finally reached another representative, I was informed that a check would be sent to me, and I was asked which of the XXXX amounts I wanted refunded. I clarified that either amount would suffice, as both were the same. Despite this, I have yet to receive the check, and the website now indicates that my payment is late, as if I never made the payments. This situation has left me without the funds to make another payment. Not sure what else to do. I work. Now I have to waste another day trying to call them with a new issue.

I am submitting this complaint as a formal escalation of a previously reported issue from XXXX XXXX, when my mortgage loan was transferred from XXXX XXXX to Mr. Cooper. At that time, Mr. Coopers payment portal failed to properly display my active loan. I filed a CFPB complaint, but the companys response did not address the root cause.

Although I was later able to make several successful payments, the identical issue has now recurred as of XX/XX/XXXX. When logging into Mr. Coopers payment portal, my current active loan does not appear. Instead, the system displays an older loan that had previously been serviced by Mr. Cooper.

This presents serious operational and consumer access concerns : 1. * * Account Access Impairment : * * The failure to display the correct active loan prevents me from making timely payments through the online system. In XXXX, this forced me to mail a payment without confirmation or idempotency safeguards.

2. * * Data Integrity and System Controls : * * The recurrence of obsolete loan data in place of the active account suggests unresolved backend data governance, merger integration, or quality assurance deficiencies. Mr. Cooper merged with XXXX XXXX, and the persistence of outdated account records raises questions regarding database synchronization and internal controls.

3. * * Lack of Effective Human Escalation : * * When the automated system fails, there is no reliable path to reach a live representative without passing flawed verification mechanisms. This creates a barrier to servicing an active mortgage obligation.

clusters.name: Credit Reporting — Late, Payments, Late Payments — Incorrect information on your report
sample_narratives:
I am submitting this complaint because multiple accounts on my credit report are showing late payments that are inaccurate, unverified, and in many cases tied to accounts I did not open, authorize, or have any knowledge of. These late-payment entries have significantly harmed my credit profile, reduced my score, created barriers to financial opportunities, and continue to appear on my credit report despite my disputes with the credit bureaus. I am requesting the Consumer Financial Protection Bureaus assistance because these accounts and their late-payment histories have not been properly investigated under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), and the furnishers have failed to provide any original documentation proving these late payments are valid or belong to me.

This complaint includes all of the accounts currently reporting late payments, including the following : XXXX XXXX ( multiple accounts ), XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX ( multiple entries ), XXXX XXXX ( multiple entries ), XXXX XXXX / XXXX XXXX, and XXXX XXXX. Across these accounts, late paymentssome showing as 30 days late, 60 days late, 90 days late, and even longerare appearing in my credit history. Many of these accounts are not mine, and many others show late-payment activity that is inaccurate, duplicated, or unsupported by any evidence. I have not received monthly statements, due notices, validation letters, or proof of delinquency for any of these accounts.

I believe these late-payment entries are the result of a combination of factors including potential identity theft, mixed-file errors, incorrect reporting by furnishers, outdated or incomplete data, automated system errors, and inadequate reinvestigation procedures by the credit bureaus. I have attempted to dispute these items individually, but the credit bureaus continue to verify them without providing any Method of Verification, copies of contracts, payment histories, or documentation confirming that I actually made late p

I am disputing the accuracy, completeness, and legal verifiability of ALL late payments XXXX  is reporting on the following accounts : Accounts in Dispute ( XXXX  Reporting ) : XXXX XXXX Acct # XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Acct # XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( {$400.00} limit ) XXXX XXXX Acct # XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( {$200.00} limit ) Basis of Dispute Accounts are reported as OPEN and CURRENT, some showing {$0.00} past due Late payments are still being reported, which is materially misleading TransUnions reporting does not match Equifax or XXXX  Information that is not reported consistently can not be accurate This constitutes violations of FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) and 623 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ). 

PROHIBITION OF e-OSCAR You are EXPRESSLY PROHIBITED from verifying this dispute through e-OSCAR or any automated system.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 14 consumers affected by the same pattern at Experian Information Solutions Inc. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 14 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dispute resolution (e-OSCAR-style) is likely closing investigations without genuine human review, leaving errors uncorrected.
hypotheses.evidence: 14 complaints showing automated-investigation and unresolved-dispute cues; concentrated at Experian Information Solutions Inc., EQUIFAX, INC., TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Experian Information Solutions Inc. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Experian Information Solutions Inc. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Student Lending — Notice, Federal, Mohela — Dealing with your lender or servicer
companies: Servicer under contract with Federal Student Aid
sample_narratives:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I logged into my IRS account to check the status of my amended refund and saw a transaction code showing my refund was applied to a non-IRS debt. This was the FIRST time I learned anything about an offset. I immediately called the Treasury XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ), and the automated system informed me that my refund had been sent to the U.S. Department of Education ( DOE ). I received no advance notice whatsoever. 

My XXXX federal tax refund was taken on XX/XX/XXXX without the legally required XXXX Notice of Intent to XXXX. Under 31 U.S.C. 3716 ( a ), 31 C.F.R. 285.5, and 34 C.F.R. 30.22, DOE must send a current written notice BEFORE each offset year, giving borrowers updated debt information and the opportunity to dispute or request a hearing. 

Because I received no notice, I was denied all due-process rights. 

Later that same day ( XX/XX/XXXX ), I contacted DOEs Default Resolution Group. DOE staff admitted that the ONLY notice they claim to have issued was from XXXX. A notice from XXXX does NOT authorize a XXXX offset. Federal law requires new notice before EACH tax year an offset occurs. 

DOE representatives gave incorrect and misleading information : One said, Theres nothing you can do now. 
Another said, A notice from XXXX still goes into effect. 
A supervisor ( XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ) incorrectly said refunds cant be returned unless facing eviction or foreclosure. 
The supervisor also said HIPAA prevents them from updating addresses, which is completely irrelevant to federal student loan servicing.

Staff suggested email notice could apply, but I received no email either. 

The Treasury XXXX letter I eventually received was mailed to my correct address, proving IRS/Treasury had my updated address. DOE failed to use the same federal address databases, violating due process ( XXXX v. XXXX  ).

I have federal student loans serviced by MOHELA. My loan was in deferment. I did not receive proper advance notice that the deferment was ending or that repayment was resuming. 

Without effective notice, MOHELA reported my account as four months delinquent to the credit bureaus. I did not intentionally miss payments and was unaware repayment had restarted.

I attempted multiple times to contact MOHELA by phone to dispute the delinquency and request clarification, but I was unable to reach a live representative due to repeated automated phone system loops.

I am disputing the accuracy of the reported delinquency, which resulted from a servicer communication failure, not borrower nonpayment after notice.

clusters.name: Auto Lending — Toyota, Financial, Toyota Financial — Repossession
sample_narratives:
I am filing a complaint against Toyota Financial Services/Toyota Motor Credit Corporation for an unauthorized withdrawal of {$720.00} from my bank account after my auto loan was already paid off and closed. 

Toyotas own automated system states : Your account closed and paid off on XX/XX/year>. 

Despite this, Toyota Financial withdrew {$720.00} from my account on XX/XX/year>, without authorization. I never approved any additional payments after my vehicle was paid off by XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

When I contacted Toyota Financial Services, I was given contradictory and misleading information. A representative XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX insisted the account was still active and refused to let me speak with a supervisor. He denied escalation multiple times, even though this involved an unauthorized withdrawal. I have been given the runaround for days and have experienced significant stress and emotional distress as a result, as well as financial hardship due to the unexpected withdrawal. 

Toyota Financial is also short {$230.00} from the original payoff check sent by XXXX XXXX XXXX, and I have not been provided any explanation for that discrepancy. 

My concerns include : Unauthorized withdrawal from a closed account Contradictory information from representatives Refusal to escalate a financial dispute Mishandling of payoff funds Emotional and financial hardship Potential unfair or deceptive practices I am requesting the CFPB require Toyota Financial Services to : Immediately refund the unauthorized {$720.00} withdrawal Correct my account to properly reflect the closed and paid-off status Address the representatives refusal to escalate Investigate Toyota Financials contradictory information and mishandling of consumer accounts In addition, I am requesting compensation for the severe distress, fear, and emotional harm this situation caused. As a single mother with a toddler, the unauthorized withdrawal and refusal to escalate left me terrified and without access to needed funds rig

Toyota financial refused to work with me after me and a significant other separated. I did not have access to the accounts and started charging me additional fees for using their automated system that worked poorly and sometimes even had to speak with somebody over the phone and would charge me an additional fee. The first fee was XXXX and then if I had to speak with a representative would be XXXX dollars for years. I have recently understood that is against the law against the consumers act. I did not know this and this was happening forever. I fell on a desperate times one thing led to another and eventually they took my vehicle. I have recently found out that what they did was illegal by charging me extra fees on top of just trying to make my car payment and they would not open or separate my car from my previous partner. Ive informed them that what they have done is actually against the law that was weeks ago. I still not have heard back from them. They have destroyed my credit. I cant even get an apartment without a cosigner now I cant get a credit card to try and rebuild my credit. I miss company has financially crippled me. They also advertise a XXXX deferment payment and I got denied for that for whatever reason. And I would like to have my life back.

A letter ( dated XX/XX/XXXX ) arrived in the mail from a debt collector : XXXX XXXX XXXX trying to collect a debt owed to Toyota Motor Credit Corporation. 

Tuesday, XX/XX/XXXX - called the Toyota Financial Services main support number on their customer service line six times between XXXX XXXX EST, but the automated system told me that their office is closed and to call back when their office is open on Monday to Friday, XXXX to XXXX. 

Wednesday, XX/XX/XXXX - call the main support number on their customer service line twice between XXXX am EST. Same issue. XXXXffice is closed and to please call back during business hours. 

Thursday, XX/XX/XXXX -- called the main support number on their customer service line 3 times around XXXXXXXX XXXX and got the same result. Instead, I tried their main line number and pressed " 0 '' until I reached a live agent, then asked to be transferred to a live agent in the Financial Services department to inquire about my account that needs attention. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX - I was transferred to FS Rep # 1, XXXX and gave me a number to call back in case we get disconnected but it was in fact the collections agency ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ). 

XXXX AM EST - When I called back, I was connected to FS Rep # XXXX, Spell ( XXXX ). She had to speak with a Supervisor regarding my request, but was told that because my account was " closed '' they could not dig up any information on this, and advised that I reach out to another number to follow-up questions, which is the same number as XXXX Systems XXXX XXXX provided by Rep # 1, XXXX. When I insisted on XXXX providing me with the documentation, statements, or notices that they have on file, XXXX put me on hold before ultimately transferring me to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX without my consent. 

XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX - When I called back and connected a FS Rep # 3, XXXX ( XXXX ). She expressed empathy for my frustration, said she would help me lodge a formal complaint against FS Rep # 2, and connected me with XXXX X

clusters.name: Auto Lending — Payoff, Attempts, Unable Reach — Managing the loan or lease
sample_narratives:
Ally sent the voluntary surrender paperwork to an incorrect address, despite my correct address being on file. I was informed by Ally representatives that I could not receive corrected paperwork and had to wait until the documents expired on XX/XX/year>. 

After XX/XX/XXXX, I still have not received the corrected paperwork, despite multiple attempts to contact Ally. When I call Allys customer service number, the automated system hangs up, and I am unable to reach a live representative or a supervisor. Ally has refused to transfer me to a supervisor.

I have made repeated good-faith efforts to complete a voluntary surrender, but Allys failure to send paperwork to the correct address and refusal to communicate has prevented me from completing the surrender. 

I am requesting that Ally immediately provide written instructions and paperwork to complete the voluntary surrender and correct their records to reflect my XXXX request. I am also requesting that Ally stop delaying this process and respond in writing. 
I have made repeated attempts to contact Ally Auto Finance by phone over multiple days.

My call logs show numerous outbound calls, including calls lasting over one hour and multiple calls that ended abruptly within minutes. Despite these efforts, I was unable to reach a supervisor, and calls frequently disconnected or ended through the automated system.

These records demonstrate my continued good-faith attempts to resolve this matter and Allys failure to provide meaningful access to customer service or supervision.

I am attempting to obtain an official payoff quote and pay off my auto loan with Westlake Financial Services Account number : XXXX Phone : XXXX Email : XXXX Car : XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX. I have made multiple attempts to contact the company by phone during their stated business hours but have been unable to reach a live representative. The automated system does not allow me to connect with a human agent to obtain a payoff amount. 

I am requesting : 1. An official 10-day payoff quote in writing.

2. Clear instructions on how to submit final payoff funds.

3. Written confirmation once the loan is paid in full.

4. Confirmation of title release procedures and timeline.

I am ready and willing to pay the full outstanding balance immediately. However, I am unable to complete the payoff due to lack of access to a live representative and inability to obtain accurate payoff information.

This delay is causing financial harm and unnecessary stress. I request prompt assistance and direct contact from Westlake Financial to resolve this matter immediately.

I purchased my leased XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX : XXXX ) at the end of the lease term. The buyout was completed on XX/XX/XXXX, and I have the Bill of Sale as proof of full payoff. 

Nearly four years later, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) has still not released the lien with the California XXXX. My current registration renewal notice ( due XX/XX/XXXX ) still shows the lienholder on record, confirming the lien was never released electronically through California 's ELT ( Electronic Lien and Title ) system. 

I have attempted to contact XXXX XXXX  by phone at XXXX on multiple occasions. Their automated system restricts callers to a chatbot with limited options, and after repeated attempts to reach a live agent, the system states that agents are too busy and to try again later. I have been unable to reach a human being to resolve this. 

Under California Vehicle Code Section 5753, a lienholder is required to release the lien within 21 days of payoff. XXXX XXXX  is nearly four years past this legal deadline. 

I am requesting that XXXX XXXX immediately release the lien electronically via California 's ELT system so that I can obtain a clean title for my vehicle.

clusters.name: Student Lending — Mohela, Access, Issue — Dealing with your lender or servicer
sample_narratives:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I received 4 delinquent payment alerts on my credit report from Firstmark XXXX XXXX. I called Firstmark and the automated response told me my account was current and not due until XX/XX/XXXX. On the XXXX, I called to find out from a representative that there was XXXX cents past due. However the automated system did not indicate any past due amount. My credit score dropped by XXXX points because of this. 

Between XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX, I received XXXX delinquent payment alerts on my credit report from Firstmark XXXX XXXX. Each time the automated call system and the online portal said my accounts were current. Payments are not due until the XXXX of each month. When I talked with a representative on the phone, they said that payment processing between Firstmark and XXXX 's bank is what was causing the delinquent alerts, even though Firstmark had the funds in their account. 

In total, my credit score went from XXXX to XXXX in a matter of 5 months. I've worked incredibly hard to maintain a health credit score. The actions of Firstmark are really unacceptable to consumers trying to manage their student debt.

My application has now remained in this final review stage for approximately two months with no estimated timeframe for completion. During this period, I have submitted multiple Feedback cases through Federal Student Aid seeking clarification and assistance. These cases were all closed with automated responses directing me only to check my status online, without providing any substantive explanation, timeline, or resolution.

I have also followed the instructions provided by the Federal Student Aid Ombudsmans automated system. Despite doing so, my concerns about the prolonged delay and lack of transparency remain unresolved.

I am not disputing eligibility or requesting a change in the substantive determination of my application. I am seeking assistance with administrative processing delays, lack of meaningful communication, and the absence of a clear timeframe for completion after approval stages have been passed.

I respectfully request that this complaint be reviewed to help facilitate transparency, accountability, and completion of the final administrative steps of my TPD discharge.

Subject : Complaint Regarding Inability to Access Account or Pay Remaining Student Loan Balance ( MOHELA ) To Whom It May Concern, I am submitting this complaint regarding MOHELA and my inability to access my student loan account or make payment on a remaining balance of {$150.00}, despite repeated good-faith efforts to resolve the issue. 

For months, I have attempted to pay this remaining balance, but I have been unable to access my account or reach anyone who can assist me. Specifically : Online account access : When I attempt to log in or verify my identity online, the system reports that my name and Social Security number do not match their records. I have only one legal name and Social Security number, and I am entering them correctly. 

Phone system : When I call MOHELA, the automated system requires me to enter the same identifying information. After doing so, it again states that my information does not match their records, preventing me from reaching a live representative for assistance. 

Email attempts : I have emailed MOHELA requesting help, but I was only redirected back to the same phone number that I can not access due to the identity verification issue. 

Federal student loan support : I also contacted the main federal student loan service to report that I do not have access to my MOHELA account and can not make payment. This did not resolve the issue. 

Cashiers check : As a final attempt to resolve the matter, I mailed MOHELA a cashiers check for the full remaining balance. The check was returned to me and not processed. 

Despite my repeated efforts to pay this debt in full, my credit score has been negatively affected for nine months due to this unresolved balance, resulting in nine missed payment docks. 

I am requesting CFPB assistance to : Ensure MOHELA properly verifies my identity and restores access to my account, OR Accept and process my payment for the full remaining balance immediately, and Correct all nine months of negative credit rep

This issue is particularly concerning because I have experienced similar servicing problems with Sallie Mae in the past. clusters.name: Student Lending — Asked, Principal, Balance — Dealing with your lender or servicer
sample_narratives:
Description of Incident : I am requesting a full discharge of my three student loans due to deceptive and predatory servicing practices by EdFinancial.

For several years, I was enrolled in EdFinancials automatic payment system. I was led to believe this was a good-faith method to satisfy my debt. However, EdFinancial structured these automated payments to cover interest only, allocating {$0.00} to the principal balance for every single payment made.

By luring me into an automated system that resulted in zero principal reduction without clear disclosure, EdFinancial has engaged in unfair and deceptive acts that effectively created a " debt trap. '' This practice of forced negative amortization has caused me significant financial harm by keeping me in a cycle of perpetual interest payments while my total balance remains unchanged.

Because EdFinancial failed in its duty to provide transparent repayment options and instead chose to maximize interest collection through deceptive automation, I am requesting that these loans be discharged in full.

Desired Resolution : Full discharge of all three loans associated with account username XXXX, a correction of my payment history to reflect principal reduction, and a formal investigation into EdFinancial 's automated payment allocation practices.

I called XXXX on XX/XX/year> at XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX. But the automated system stated " unable to find your records '' based off my phone number and social security number. Then, another message stated to call " Navient '' if your loans was originally managed by Navient and transferred to XXXX. Next, I called Navient at XXXX. I was on the phone with representative " XXXX for XXXX minutes. '' I asked why my XXXX loan for XXXX University XXXX which has the first disbursement amount of XXXX wasn't configured to the balance for XXXX ( because XXXX was a cancelled amount ). I asked Navient why the principal balance of XXXX of final disbursement FROM XXXX was this total? Navient stated " because of your capitalized FORBERANCE. Again, she stated, because of XXXX. But I am not in forbearance. I have been in XXXX. I asked her how am I going to pay, or try to pay when you put me in forbearance and the interest is so high? I AM IN XXXX. I have been in XXXX the past few years. I AM NOT IN XXXX. So now, a dispersed loan of XXXX is now, according to their records is XXXX because of forbearance interest. So all this time, they had me in forbearance and not XXXX. So the principal balance XXXX from an original disbursement of XXXX is now XXXX??? Ask her how am I ever going to pay when the interest is more than the amount borrowed because you based it off of forberance???

On XX/XX/XXXX, I applied on XXXX for an IDR repayment plan. Afterwards, I called and spoke with ED-Financial at the hours of XXXX to XXXX where I confirmed my IDR had placed me into an administrative forbearance, as well as requesting 3 months of deferment. The representative who I spoke with ( over a recorded line ), informed me that my account was eligible for three months of deferment. 

After a few months, I decided to check XXXX to see the status of my student loans. I noticed my IDR had been completed, and that I had no past due balance. I was very confused on why XXXX wasn't updating. XXXX claimed that due to the OBBBA, accounts under IDR and SAVE are in an administrative backlog. Of course, mine were apart of that backlog. I tried to make a payment in XXXX, but found no way to complete the payment process online. I tried calling Ed-Financial when I was available after work in XXXX, but they were closed. The automated system claimed I was late on my payments. I called once again when they were open, where they informed me that my payments were 110 days late as of XX/XX/XXXX. That would mean that my first payment was due on XX/XX/XXXX. Assuming my accounts were in administrative forbearance for one month, that would be XX/XX/XXXX, plus 3 months of deferment. My first payment should've been due in XXXX of XXXX. 

When I brought this up with a representative at Ed-Financial on XX/XX/XXXX, they told me they had no record of my request for deferment. I asked them for all relevant recordings and calls log from that specific date, which they record and are required to retain. They declined.

actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Student Lending — Reporting, Delinquency, Delinquency Reporting — Incorrect information on your report
sample_narratives:
I am submitting this complaint regarding my XXXX  student loan account serviced by XXXX. 

Background : My loans were transferred to XXXX, and during the transfer and afterward, I experienced ongoing technical issues accessing my online account. Specifically, XXXX website would not allow me to move forward beyond a particular screen, preventing me from logging in, viewing account details, or making payments. This occurred from XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX, and the issues persist to this day. 

Actions Taken : On XX/XX/XXXX, I sent a message to XXXX requesting retroactive administrative forbearance and correction of delinquency reporting for the XXXX period. I was unable to screenshot this initial message. I was able to capture a screenshot of CRIs response, in which they acknowledged my request but refused, claiming they can not remove reporting if it was accurate. 

During this chat session, the system froze and logged me out again before I could complete the conversation. 

Issue : The delinquency reporting for XXXX XXXX was caused by servicer-related access issues, not a refusal or neglect to pay. My account is currently fully up to date, with all payments made on time. Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), XXXX, as the data furnisher, is responsible for ensuring that information reported to the credit bureaus is accurate and complete. Reporting delinquencies that resulted from servicer system failures is inaccurate. 

Request : XXXX. Apply retroactive administrative forbearance for XXXX XXXX. 
XXXX. Correct any delinquency reporting caused by these system access issues. 
XXXX. Provide written confirmation of actions taken. 

Supporting Documentation : Screenshot of XXXX chat acknowledging my request and refusal.

I am filing this complaint regarding inaccurate delinquency reporting and servicing failures by MOHELA related to my student loan account. 
Auto-debit was requested and established in XX/XX/XXXX using my XXXX XXXX account ending in XXXX ( see attached statement for reference ). My account was set up for automatic monthly payments. 
Between XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, payments were not drafted from my account. During this time, I did not receive any failed draft notifications, returned payment alerts, or delinquency warnings. I was unaware that payments were not being processed. 
In XX/XX/XXXX, after learning of similar issues from a coworker, I logged into my account to review my status and discovered that payments had not been deducted as expected. Only after logging in did I begin receiving delinquency email notifications. It appeared that notifications resumed or were triggered following my login. 
Once I realized the issue, I immediately sent manual payment to bring the account fully current. I also re-established auto-debit using a different bank account, my XXXX account ending in XXXX, to prevent further issues. 
Despite being enrolled in auto-debit and not receiving failed draft notifications during XXXX XXXX, MOHELA reported XXXX account as delinquent to the credit bureaus. The account is now fully current and remains in good standing. 
I have attempted to resolve this issue directly with MOHELA on XXXX separate occasions prior to filing this complaint, requesting investigation and correction of the delinquency reporting. My requests were denied. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, at approximately XXXX XXXX, I spoke with a MOHELA representative identified as XXXX ( ID : XXXX ). During this call, the representative raised his voice, attempted to belittle me regarding monitoring my auto-debit, blamed me for not checking emails, denied the possibility of a system error, and initially refused to escalate my call to a supervisor, stating that a supervisor would tell me the same thing. I w

I am submitting this complaint because MOHELA, acting as the federal student loan servicer for the Department of Education, continues to report inaccurate and legally unverifiable late payment information on my credit reports despite receiving multiple disputes and formal requests for verification. 

The accounts involved in this complaint are : MOHELA / XXXX XXXX MOHELA / XXXX  XXXX MOHELA / XXXX  XXXX MOHELA / XXXX  XXXX These student loan accounts themselves are not being disputed. My complaint specifically concerns the late payment reporting associated with these accounts, which is inaccurate, inconsistent across credit bureaus, and unsupported by the documentation required under federal consumer protection laws. 

I have repeatedly notified MOHELA and the credit reporting agencies that the late payment data associated with these student loans is materially inconsistent across XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. The same accounts are reporting different delinquency severities and timelines depending on the bureau reviewing the data. In some cases, the accounts show conflicting XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX late designations for the same periods of time. 

A single federal student loan can not legitimately generate multiple conflicting delinquency histories across credit bureaus. The presence of these contradictions demonstrates that the information being furnished does not meet the legal standard of maximum possible accuracy required under federal law. 

In addition to the conflicting delinquency timelines, the accounts also reflect inconsistent reporting regarding payment history, activity dates, and other servicing information. These discrepancies indicate that the information being furnished is not being verified through actual servicing records. 

Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, once a consumer disputes information, the furnisher of that information has a legal obligation to conduct a reasonable investigation using the underlying records that support the reporting.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 3 consumers affected by the same pattern at MOHELA and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated adverse actions.
The pattern repeats across 3 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.

hypotheses.mechanism: A recently deployed or changed automated decisioning process appears to be producing the recurring detriment seen in these complaints.
hypotheses.evidence: 3 complaints showing repeating automated-handling cues; concentrated at MOHELA, Central Research Inc.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to MOHELA for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask MOHELA directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.

clusters.name: Auto Lending — Proof, Vin, Need — Getting a loan or lease
sample_narratives:
Upstart 's automated system is incorrectly decoding my VIN ( XXXX ) as a XXXX XXXX XXXX. The XXXX digit of my VIN is " J '', which is the federal legal identifier for a XXXX model year. I have the state-issued registration and XXXX verification XXXX it is a XXXX. 

When I attempted to correct this, representatives were verbally abusive, told me it was " my fault, '' and repeatedly hung up on me. They then allowed my application to expire today while I was actively attempting to provide proof of their internal data error. I believe my phone number has been blocked to prevent me from disputing this inaccurate information. This is a violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) regarding the use of accurate data.

On these dates XX/XX/XXXX XX/XX/XXXX XX/XX/XXXX XX/XX/XXXX I see inquiries from Westlake that I didnt authorize them to do. I contacted them on XX/XX/XXXX but it was all automated system. I need these inquiries removed because I didnt authorize them nor do they have any proof or provided any proof.
